        if std::path::Path::new("/etc/systemd/system/{internal_name}.service").exists()
            || std::path::Path::new("/Library/LaunchDaemons/{plist_name}.plist").exists()
            || std::path::Path::new("/usr/local/etc/rc.d/{internal_name}").exists()
        {
            return !is_elevated();
        }
        false
    }
}

// Port binding: check if privileged
fn needs_escalation_for_port(port: u16) -> bool {
    if port >= 1024 {
        return false;
    }
    #[cfg(windows)]
    return false;
    #[cfg(unix)]
    return !is_elevated();
}

// Update: check if binary is writable
fn needs_escalation_for_update() -> bool {
    let binary_path = match std::env::current_exe() {
        Ok(p) => p,
        Err(_) => return true,
    };
    std::fs::OpenOptions::new()
        .write(true)
        .open(&binary_path)
        .is_err()
}

// Backup: check directory access (no auth needed)
fn can_backup() -> bool {
    is_writable(&get_backup_dir())
}

// Setup: check authorization (not just access)
fn can_setup(token: &str) -> (bool, &'static str) {
    if is_database_empty() {
        return (true, "first-run");
    }
    if is_elevated() {
        return (true, "elevated");
    }
    if !token.is_empty() && validate_setup_token(token) {
        return (true, "token");
    }
    (false, "")
}

// Restore: check authorization (destructive operation)
fn can_restore() -> (bool, &'static str) {
    if is_database_empty() {
        return (true, "empty");
    }
    if is_elevated() {
        return (true, "elevated");
    }
    if is_service_user() {
        return (false, "need-creds");
    }
    (false, "denied")
}

// Mode change: check authorization
fn can_change_mode() -> (bool, &'static str) {
    if is_elevated() {
        return (true, "elevated");
    }
    if is_service_user() {
        return (false, "need-creds");
    }
    (false, "denied")
}
```

**Smart escalation behavior:**

```
User runs: {project_name} --service --install

Binary checks:
в”ңв”Җ Already root/admin? вҶ’ Proceed with system service
в”ңв”Җ Can escalate (in sudo/wheel/admin group)?
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ YES вҶ’ Prompt: "Install system service? This requires sudo. [Y/n]"
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ        в”ңв”Җ Y вҶ’ Re-exec with sudo, install system service
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ        в””в”Җ N вҶ’ "Installing user service instead..."
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җ NO вҶ’ "You don't have admin access. Installing user service..."
в””в”Җ Install appropriate service type
```

**Port fallback behavior:**

```
User runs: {project_name} --port 80

Binary checks:
в”ңв”Җ Already root/admin? вҶ’ Bind port 80, proceed
в”ңв”Җ Can escalate?
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ YES вҶ’ Prompt: "Port 80 requires sudo. Escalate? [Y/n]"
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ        в”ңв”Җ Y вҶ’ Re-exec with sudo
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ        в””в”Җ N вҶ’ "Using random port 64xxx instead..."
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җ NO вҶ’ "Port 80 requires admin. Using random port 64xxx instead..."
в””в”Җ Warn user of actual port in use
```

#### The `{project_name}` System User/Group

**Created automatically during first root/service startup.**

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| **Username** | `{project_name}` |
| **Group** | `{project_name}` |
| **Shell** | `/usr/sbin/nologin` (no login) |
| **Home** | `/var/lib/{project_org}/{internal_name}` |
| **UID/GID** | Auto-assigned by system |
| **Type** | System user (UID < 1000 on Linux) |

**What the `{project_name}` user CAN do:**

| Permission | Details |
|------------|---------|
| Read/write config | `/etc/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` |
| Read/write data | `/var/lib/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` |
| Read/write cache | `/var/cache/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` |
| Read/write logs | `/var/log/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` |
| Read/write backups | `/var/lib/Backups/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` or `/mnt/Backups/...` |
| Use bound sockets | Inherited from root before privilege drop |
| Bind ports >1024 | New sockets after privilege drop |
| Run scheduled tasks | Backup, cleanup, SSL renewal, etc. |
| Manage database | SQLite in data dir |
| Manage SSL certs | In `{config_dir}/ssl/` |

**What the `{project_name}` user CANNOT do:**

| Restriction | Reason |
|-------------|--------|
| Bind ports <1024 | Already bound before drop, or not allowed |
| Modify service files | `/etc/systemd/`, `/Library/LaunchDaemons/` owned by root |
| Create/delete system users | Requires root |
| Access other users' data | Standard Unix permissions |
| Write to `/usr`, `/bin`, etc. | System directories |
| Modify the binary | In system path, owned by root |
| Install updates | Binary replacement requires root |

**Directory ownership (set by root before privilege drop):**

```bash
# Binary sets these during startup as root
chown -R {internal_name}:{internal_name} /etc/{project_org}/{internal_name}/
chown -R {internal_name}:{internal_name} /var/lib/{project_org}/{internal_name}/
chown -R {internal_name}:{internal_name} /var/cache/{project_org}/{internal_name}/
chown -R {internal_name}:{internal_name} /var/log/{project_org}/{internal_name}/

# Permissions
chmod 755 /etc/{project_org}/{internal_name}/
chmod 700 /etc/{project_org}/{internal_name}/security/
chmod 700 /etc/{project_org}/{internal_name}/ssl/
chmod 700 /etc/{project_org}/{internal_name}/tor/
chmod 755 /var/lib/{project_org}/{internal_name}/
chmod 755 /var/cache/{project_org}/{internal_name}/
chmod 755 /var/log/{project_org}/{internal_name}/
```

**User creation:** See PART 24 for platform-specific user creation commands (Linux `useradd`, macOS `dscl`, FreeBSD `pw`).

**User service vs system service:**

| Aspect | System Service | User Service |
|--------|---------------|--------------|
| **Installed by** | root/admin | Regular user |
| **Runs as** | root вҶ’ drops to `{project_name}` | Calling user |
| **Ports** | Any | >1024 only |
| **Paths** | `/etc/`, `/var/` | `~/.config/`, `~/.local/` |
| **Survives logout** | Yes | Depends on `lingering` |
| **Auto-start** | On boot | On user login |
| **Service file** | `/etc/systemd/system/` | `~/.config/systemd/user/` |

### Port Display Rules

| Rule | Description |
|------|-------------|
| Strip `:80` | Don't show port for HTTP on 80 |
| Strip `:443` | Don't show port for HTTPS on 443 |
| Always show others | Show port for all non-standard ports |

### Dual Port Support

**Applications can listen on two ports simultaneously for HTTP and HTTPS:**

| Format | Description | Example |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| Single | One port (HTTP; exception: port 443 = HTTPS-only; `ssl.enabled` overrides) | `8090` |
| Dual | HTTP port, HTTPS port (comma-separated) | `8090,8443` |

**Dual Port Behavior:**

| HTTP Port | HTTPS Port | Behavior |
|-----------|------------|----------|
| Any | Any | Both ports active, HTTP redirects to HTTPS optional |
| 80 | 443 | Let's Encrypt challenges enabled, standard web ports |
| 64xxx | 64xxx+1 | Common pattern for random ports |

```yaml
# Single port (HTTP by default; exception: port 443 = HTTPS-only mode; ssl.enabled overrides)
server:
  port: 8090

# Single port with ssl.enabled override (HTTPS on non-443 port)
# Forces HTTPS on port 8090
server:
  port: 8090
  ssl:
    enabled: true

# Dual port (HTTP on 8090, HTTPS on 8443)
server:
  port: "8090,8443"
  ssl:
    enabled: true

# Standard web ports
server:
  port: "80,443"
  ssl:
    enabled: true
    letsencrypt:
      enabled: true
```

### Configuration

```yaml
server:
  # Port options:
  # - Omit or empty: Random port in 64000-64999 range
  # - Single number: Use that exact port
  # - Dual (HTTP,HTTPS): "8090,8443" format
  # - 0: Let OS assign any available port
  port: 64580
```

### Admin Panel

Port can be changed via `/server/{admin_path}/config/settings`, but **requires server restart** (with warning shown to user).

### Example Structure

```yaml
# =============================================================================
# SERVER CONFIGURATION
# =============================================================================

server:
  # Default: random unused port in 64xxx range
  port: {random}
  # Auto-detected from host
  fqdn: {hostname}
  # [::] = all interfaces IPv4/IPv6
  address: "[::]"
  # production or development
  mode: production
  # Admin panel path (default: admin) - see PART 17
  admin_path: admin
  # API version prefix (default: v1) - used in /api/{api_version}/ routes
  api_version: v1
  healthz:
    root:
      # Optional root compatibility alias. Canonical route stays /server/healthz.
      # When true, mount /healthz to the SAME handler as /server/healthz (never redirect).
      enabled: false

  # Branding & SEO - see PART 16 for full details
  branding:
    title: "{project_name}"
    tagline: ""
    description: ""
  seo:
    keywords: []

  # System user/group
  user: {auto}
  group: {auto}

  # PID file
  pidfile: true

  # Daemonize on start (detach from terminal)
  # Default: false (modern service managers prefer foreground)
  daemonize: false

  # Admin Panel
  admin:
    email: admin@{fqdn}
    # Note: username, password, and token are stored in database (admins table)
    # NOT in this config file for security

  # SSL/TLS
  ssl:
    enabled: false
    # cert/key: Optional manual override paths (leave empty for auto-detection)
    # Auto-detection order:
    #   /etc/letsencrypt/live/domain/ вҶ’ system manages (certbot)
    #   /etc/letsencrypt/live/{fqdn}/ вҶ’ system manages (certbot)
    #   {config_dir}/ssl/letsencrypt/{fqdn}/ вҶ’ app manages (auto-renew)
    #   {config_dir}/ssl/local/{fqdn}/ вҶ’ user manages (no auto-renew)
    cert: ""   # Manual cert path (optional)
    key: ""    # Manual key path (optional)
    min_version: "TLS1.2"  # TLS1.2, TLS1.3

    letsencrypt:
      enabled: false
      email: admin@{fqdn}
      challenge: http-01  # http-01, tls-alpn-01, dns-01
      staging: false      # Use staging server for testing

  # Scheduler - manages all background tasks
  scheduler:
    enabled: true
    # Built-in tasks with sane defaults (all enabled by default)
    tasks:
      # Security database updates
      geoip_update:
        enabled: true
        # Weekly: Sunday 3:00 AM
        schedule: "0 3 * * 0"
        retry_on_fail: true
        retry_delay: 1h
      blocklist_update:
        enabled: true
        # Daily: 4:00 AM
        schedule: "0 4 * * *"
        retry_on_fail: true
        retry_delay: 1h
      cve_update:
        enabled: true
        # Daily: 5:00 AM
        schedule: "0 5 * * *"
        retry_on_fail: true
        retry_delay: 1h

      # Maintenance tasks
      log_rotation:
        enabled: true
        # Daily: midnight
        schedule: "0 0 * * *"
        max_age: 30d
        max_size: 100MB
      session_cleanup:
        enabled: true
        # Hourly
        schedule: "@hourly"
      backup:
        enabled: true
        # Daily: 2:00 AM
        schedule: "0 2 * * *"
        # Keep max 4 backups (storage management)
        retention: 4

      # SSL certificate management (only when app manages certs)
      ssl_renewal:
        enabled: true
        # Daily: 3:00 AM (after backup at 2:00)
        schedule: "0 3 * * *"
        # Renew 7 days before expiry
        renew_before: 7d

      # Health checks
      health_check:
        enabled: true
        # Every 5 minutes
        schedule: "*/5 * * * *"

      # Tor maintenance
      tor_health:
        enabled: true
        # Every 10 minutes
        schedule: "*/10 * * * *"

  rate_limit:
    enabled: true
    # Project-specific default (define in IDEA.md based on expected usage)
    requests: 0  # 0 = use project default
    window: 60

  # Database
  database:
    driver: file

# =============================================================================
# FRONTEND CONFIGURATION
# =============================================================================

web:
  ui:
    theme: dark
  cors: "*"
```

---

# CHECKPOINT 4: CONFIGURATION VERIFICATION

Before proceeding, confirm you understand:
- [ ] Config file is `server.yml` (not .yaml)
- [ ] Boolean handling uses `config::parse_bool()` - NEVER `str::parse::<bool>()`
- [ ] All boolean inputs accept truthy/falsy values (yes, enable, oui, etc.)
- [ ] Environment variables: some runtime, some init-only
- [ ] Config auto-created on first run with sane defaults

---


# PART 6: APPLICATION MODES

## Mode and Debug Detection Priority

**Mode:**
1. `--mode` CLI flag (highest priority)
2. `MODE` environment variable
3. Default: `production`

**Debug:**
1. `--debug` CLI flag (highest priority)
2. `DEBUG` environment variable (truthy values)
3. Default: `false`

## Three Operational States

| State | Mode | Debug | Use Case |
|-------|------|-------|----------|
| **Production** | `production` | `false` | Live deployment, no debugging |
| **Production + Debug** | `production` | `true` | Live debugging (temporary) |
| **Development** | `development` | `false` | Local development, sensible defaults |
| **Development + Debug** | `development` | `true` | Full debugging, all features |

## Production Mode (Default)

| Setting | Behavior |
|---------|----------|
| Logging | `info` level, minimal output |
| Debug endpoints | **Disabled** (`/debug/*` returns 404) |
| tokio-console | **Disabled** |
| Error messages | Generic (no stack traces) |
| Panic recovery | Graceful (logs error, returns 500) |
| Template caching | Enabled |
| Static file caching | Enabled |
| Rate limiting | Enforced |
| Security headers | All enabled |
| Sensitive data | Never shown |
| Request logging | Minimal (status, duration) |

## Development Mode

**Enables sensible debugging for local development, but NOT full debug endpoints.**

| Setting | Behavior |
|---------|----------|
| Logging | `debug` level, verbose |
| Debug endpoints | **Disabled** (use `--debug` to enable) |
| tokio-console | **Disabled** (use `--debug` to enable) |
| Error messages | Detailed (stack traces in logs) |
| Panic recovery | Verbose (full stack in response) |
| Template caching | Disabled (hot reload) |
| Static file caching | Disabled (hot reload) |
| Rate limiting | Relaxed/disabled |
| Security headers | Relaxed (CORS permissive) |
| Sensitive data | Can be shown (with warning) |
| Request logging | Verbose (headers, body preview) |

## Debug Flag (`--debug` / `DEBUG=true`)

**Enables ALL debug features regardless of mode. Use sparingly in production.**

| Setting | Behavior |
|---------|----------|
| **Admin authentication** | **BYPASSED** (for manual dev only - NOT for automated tests) |
| Debug endpoints | **Enabled** (`/debug/*`) |
| tokio-console | **Enabled** (`/debug/tokio-console`) |
| Metrics endpoints | **Enabled** (`/debug/metrics`) |
| Request/response logging | Full (headers, body, timing) |
| Database query logging | Enabled (queries, timing, rows) || Cache operation logging | Enabled (hits, misses, evictions) |
| Memory profiling | Enabled (via tokio-console / flamegraph) |
| Task monitoring | Enabled (tokio task count) |

**Console output when debug enabled:**
```
рҹ”’ Running in mode: production [debugging]
рҹ”§ Running in mode: development [debugging]
```

## Mode Shortcuts

| Shortcut | Mode |
|----------|------|
| `--mode dev` | development |
| `--mode development` | development |
| `--mode prod` | production |
| `--mode production` | production |

## Debug Endpoints (`--debug` / `DEBUG=true` Only)

**Debug endpoints are ONLY available when `--debug` flag or `DEBUG=true` is set. Otherwise returns 404.**

### Profiling Endpoints

| Endpoint | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `/debug/flamegraph` | CPU flamegraph (via flamegraph crate) |
| `/debug/tokio` | Tokio task metrics (via tokio-console) |
| `/debug/heap` | Heap allocation profile |

### Debug API Endpoints

| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |
|----------|--------|---------|
| `/debug/vars` | GET | Runtime metrics (metrics + metrics-exporter-prometheus) |
| `/debug/config` | GET | Current configuration (sanitized) |
| `/debug/routes` | GET | All registered routes |
| `/debug/cache` | GET | Cache statistics |
| `/debug/db` | GET | Database statistics |
| `/debug/scheduler` | GET | Scheduler task status |

### Debug Implementation

```rust
// src/server/debug.rs

use axum::{
    extract::State,
    response::IntoResponse,
    routing::get,
    Json, Router,
};
use std::sync::Arc;

use crate::state::AppState;

/// Build debug router вҖ” only mounted when debug mode is enabled
pub fn debug_router(state: Arc<AppState>) -> Router {
    if !state.config.is_debug() {
        return Router::new();
    }

    Router::new()
        .route("/debug/vars", get(handle_debug_vars))
        .route("/debug/config", get(handle_debug_config))
        .route("/debug/routes", get(handle_debug_routes))
        .route("/debug/cache", get(handle_debug_cache))
        .route("/debug/db", get(handle_debug_db))
        .route("/debug/scheduler", get(handle_debug_scheduler))
        .route("/debug/memory", get(handle_debug_memory))
        .route("/debug/tasks", get(handle_debug_tasks))
        .with_state(state)
}

/// Returns sanitized configuration
async fn handle_debug_config(State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>) -> impl IntoResponse {
    let sanitized = state.config.sanitized();
    Json(sanitized)
}

/// Returns all registered routes
async fn handle_debug_routes(State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>) -> impl IntoResponse {
    let routes = state.route_listing.load();
    Json(serde_json::json!({
        "count": routes.len(),
        "routes": *routes,
    }))
}

/// Returns memory and async runtime statistics
async fn handle_debug_memory(State(_state): State<Arc<AppState>>) -> impl IntoResponse {
    let metrics = tokio::runtime::Handle::current().metrics();
    Json(serde_json::json!({
        "tokio_alive_tasks": metrics.num_alive_tasks(),
        "tokio_blocking_threads": metrics.num_blocking_threads(),
        "tokio_workers": metrics.num_workers(),
        "tokio_remote_schedules": metrics.remote_schedule_count(),
    }))
}

/// Returns tokio task count
async fn handle_debug_tasks(State(_state): State<Arc<AppState>>) -> impl IntoResponse {
    let metrics = tokio::runtime::Handle::current().metrics();
    Json(serde_json::json!({
        "alive_tasks": metrics.num_alive_tasks(),
        "blocking_threads": metrics.num_blocking_threads(),
    }))
}

/// Returns Prometheus-format runtime metrics
async fn handle_debug_vars(State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>) -> impl IntoResponse {
    let snapshot = state.metrics_snapshot().await;
    Json(snapshot)
}

/// Returns cache statistics
async fn handle_debug_cache(State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>) -> impl IntoResponse {
    let stats = state.cache.stats().await;
    Json(stats)
}

/// Returns sqlx pool statistics
async fn handle_debug_db(State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>) -> impl IntoResponse {
    let pool = &state.db;
    Json(serde_json::json!({
        "size": pool.size(),
        "idle": pool.num_idle(),
        "max_connections": pool.options().get_max_connections(),
    }))
}

/// Returns scheduler task status
async fn handle_debug_scheduler(State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>) -> impl IntoResponse {
    let tasks = state.scheduler.status().await;
    Json(tasks)
}
```

### Debug Logging

```rust
// src/server/debug_log.rs

use std::time::Duration;
use tracing::debug;

/// Logs detailed request information вҖ” no-op unless debug is enabled
pub fn debug_log_request(
    method: &str,
    path: &str,
    query: &str,
    status: u16,
    duration: Duration,
    size: usize,
    remote_addr: &str,
    user_agent: &str,
    referer: &str,
    request_id: &str,
    is_debug: bool,
) {
    if !is_debug {
        return;
    }
    debug!(
        method,
        path,
        query,
        status,
        duration_ms = duration.as_millis(),
        size,
        remote_addr,
        user_agent,
        referer,
        request_id,
        "request"
    );
}

/// Logs database queries вҖ” no-op unless debug is enabled
pub fn debug_log_db(
    query: &str,
    args: &[&dyn std::fmt::Debug],
    duration: Duration,
    err: Option<&sqlx::Error>,
    is_debug: bool,
) {
    if !is_debug {
        return;
    }
    if let Some(e) = err {
        debug!(
            query,
            duration_ms = duration.as_millis(),
            error = %e,
            "db query failed"
        );
    } else {
        debug!(
            query,
            duration_ms = duration.as_millis(),
            args = ?args,
            "db query"
        );
    }
}

/// Logs cache operations вҖ” no-op unless debug is enabled
pub fn debug_log_cache(op: &str, key: &str, hit: bool, duration: Duration, is_debug: bool) {
    if !is_debug {
        return;
    }
    debug!(
        operation = op,
        key,
        hit,
        duration_us = duration.as_micros(),
        "cache"
    );
}
```

### Debug Middleware

```rust
// src/middleware/debug.rs

use axum::{
    body::Body,
    extract::Request,
    middleware::Next,
    response::Response,
};
use std::time::Instant;

use crate::server::debug_log::debug_log_request;

/// Tower middleware layer that logs detailed request/response info
/// when debug mode is enabled. No-op when debug is disabled.
pub async fn debug_middleware(
    is_debug: bool,
    req: Request,
    next: Next,
) -> Response {
    if !is_debug {
        return next.run(req).await;
    }

    let method = req.method().to_string();
    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();
    let query = req.uri().query().unwrap_or("").to_string();
    let remote_addr = req
        .extensions()
        .get::<axum::extract::ConnectInfo<std::net::SocketAddr>>()
        .map(|ci| ci.0.to_string())
        .unwrap_or_default();
    let user_agent = req
        .headers()
        .get("user-agent")
        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
        .unwrap_or("")
        .to_string();
    let referer = req
        .headers()
        .get("referer")
        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
        .unwrap_or("")
        .to_string();
    let request_id = req
        .headers()
        .get("x-request-id")
        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
        .unwrap_or("")
        .to_string();

    let start = Instant::now();
    let response = next.run(req).await;
    let duration = start.elapsed();

    let status = response.status().as_u16();
    let size = response
        .headers()
        .get("content-length")
        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
        .and_then(|v| v.parse::<usize>().ok())
        .unwrap_or(0);

    debug_log_request(
        &method, &path, &query, status, duration, size,
        &remote_addr, &user_agent, &referer, &request_id, true,
    );

    response
}
```

### Metrics Registration

```rust
// src/server/metrics.rs

use metrics::{counter, gauge, histogram};
use metrics_exporter_prometheus::PrometheusBuilder;
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};

/// Install the Prometheus metrics exporter at startup
pub fn install_metrics_exporter() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
    PrometheusBuilder::new()
        .install()
        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("failed to install metrics exporter: {e}"))
}

/// Record a completed request
pub fn record_request(duration: Duration, status: u16) {
    counter!("requests_total").increment(1);
    histogram!("requests_duration_seconds").record(duration.as_secs_f64());
    if status >= 500 {
        counter!("errors_total").increment(1);
    }
}

/// Record an application error
pub fn record_error() {
    counter!("errors_total").increment(1);
}

/// Publish uptime gauge (call periodically from a background task)
pub fn update_uptime_gauge(start: Instant) {
    gauge!("uptime_seconds").set(start.elapsed().as_secs_f64());
}
```

### Using Profiling Tools (Commands)

```bash
# Install flamegraph support
cargo install flamegraph

# CPU flamegraph (run server with debug, then profile)
sudo cargo flamegraph --bin {project_name}

# tokio-console (real-time async task inspector)
cargo install tokio-console
# Run server with TOKIO_CONSOLE=1
TOKIO_CONSOLE=1 ./{project_name} --debug
tokio-console

# cargo-instruments (macOS only)
cargo install cargo-instruments
cargo instruments -t time

# Prometheus metrics scrape
curl -q -LSsf http://localhost:64580/debug/vars

# Heap profiling with heaptrack (Linux)
heaptrack ./{project_name} --debug
heaptrack_gui heaptrack.{project_name}.*.gz
```

### Debug Configuration

```yaml
server:
  # Application mode
  mode: development  # Enables all debug features

  # Debug-specific settings (only apply in development mode)
  debug:
    # Enable flamegraph/tokio-console endpoints
    profiling: true

    # Log all SQL queries
    log_queries: true

    # Log cache operations
    log_cache: true

    # Log request/response bodies (up to max_body_log_size)
    log_bodies: false
    max_body_log_size: 10KB

    # Enable runtime/debug endpoints
    runtime_endpoints: true
```

### Debug Mode Detection

```rust
// src/mode/mod.rs

use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, AtomicU8, Ordering};

static CURRENT_MODE: AtomicU8 = AtomicU8::new(0);
static DEBUG_ENABLED: AtomicBool = AtomicBool::new(false);

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
#[repr(u8)]
pub enum AppMode {
    Production = 0,
    Development = 1,
}

impl std::fmt::Display for AppMode {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        match self {
            AppMode::Development => write!(f, "development"),
            AppMode::Production => write!(f, "production"),
        }
    }
}

/// Set application mode from a string value
pub fn set_app_mode(m: &str) {
    let mode = match m.to_lowercase().as_str() {
        "dev" | "development" => AppMode::Development,
        _ => AppMode::Production,
    };
    CURRENT_MODE.store(mode as u8, Ordering::Relaxed);
    update_profiling_settings();
}

/// Enable or disable debug mode
pub fn set_debug_enabled(enabled: bool) {
    DEBUG_ENABLED.store(enabled, Ordering::Relaxed);
    update_profiling_settings();
}

/// Apply profiling settings based on current debug state
fn update_profiling_settings() {
    // tokio-console and flamegraph are enabled via feature flags and
    // environment variables (TOKIO_CONSOLE=1), not runtime calls
}

/// Return the current application mode
pub fn get_current_app_mode() -> AppMode {
    match CURRENT_MODE.load(Ordering::Relaxed) {
        1 => AppMode::Development,
        _ => AppMode::Production,
    }
}

pub fn is_app_mode_dev() -> bool {
    get_current_app_mode() == AppMode::Development
}

pub fn is_app_mode_prod() -> bool {
    get_current_app_mode() == AppMode::Production
}

pub fn is_debug_enabled() -> bool {
    DEBUG_ENABLED.load(Ordering::Relaxed)
}

/// Return mode string with optional debug suffix
pub fn get_app_mode_string() -> String {
    let s = get_current_app_mode().to_string();
    if is_debug_enabled() {
        format!("{s} [debugging]")
    } else {
        s
    }
}

/// Initialize mode and debug from environment variables
pub fn from_env() {
    if let Ok(m) = std::env::var("MODE") {
        set_app_mode(&m);
    }
    if std::env::var("DEBUG")
        .ok()
        .map(|v| is_truthy(&v))
        .unwrap_or(false)
    {
        set_debug_enabled(true);
    }
}

fn is_truthy(s: &str) -> bool {
    matches!(s.to_lowercase().as_str(), "1" | "true" | "yes" | "on")
}
```

---


# PART 7: BINARY REQUIREMENTS

## Single Static Binary

| Requirement | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| Type | **SINGLE STATIC BINARY** |
| Assets | Embedded using `rust-embed` |
| Dependencies | None at runtime |
| Build | `cargo build --release --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl` |
| Libraries | Pure Rust only (no unsafe FFI to C) |

## Default Behavior

| Behavior | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| No arguments | Initialize (if needed) and start server |
| First run | Auto-create config with defaults, show banner with URLs and setup token |
| First run | Auto-create required directories |
| Double-click (Windows) | Console window opens, server runs in foreground with banner |
| Signals | Proper handling (SIGTERM, SIGINT, SIGHUP) |
| PID file | Enabled by default |

**See PART 17 "First Run & Setup Wizard" for server first-run experience.**
**See PART 33 "First-Run / Double-Click Behavior" for CLI setup wizard (CLI is the only binary with a setup wizard).**

## Embedded Assets

| Asset Type | Location |
|------------|----------|
| Templates | `src/server/template/` |
| Static files | `src/server/static/` |
| Application data | `src/data/` (JSON files) |

## External Data (NOT Embedded)

**Security databases are NEVER embedded in the binary.** They are downloaded on first run and kept updated via the built-in scheduler.

| Data Type | Location | Source | Update Frequency |
|-----------|----------|--------|------------------|
| GeoIP (ASN) | `{data_dir}/security/geoip/` | ip-location-db | Daily |
| GeoIP (Country) | `{data_dir}/security/geoip/` | ip-location-db | Daily |
| GeoIP (City) | `{data_dir}/security/geoip/` | ip-location-db | Daily |
| GeoIP (WHOIS) | `{data_dir}/security/geoip/` | ip-location-db | Daily |
| IP Blocklists | `{data_dir}/security/blocklists/` | Configurable sources | Daily |
| Domain Blocklists | `{data_dir}/security/blocklists/` | Configurable sources | Daily |
| CVE databases | `{data_dir}/security/cve/` | NVD/NIST feeds | Daily |
| Trivy DB | `{data_dir}/security/trivy/` | Aqua Security | Daily |

**Why NOT Embedded:**
- Security data changes frequently (daily/weekly updates)
- Embedding would require new binary releases for data updates
- Allows immediate security updates without redeployment
- Reduces binary size significantly

**Download Behavior:**
1. On first run, check if data exists in `{data_dir}/security/`
2. If missing, download from configured sources
3. If download fails, log warning and continue (graceful degradation)
4. Scheduler keeps data updated automatically

### Default Data Sources

```yaml
# Data source configuration (in server.yml)
data:
  # Base directory for all security databases
  security_dir: "{data_dir}/security"

  geoip:
    # ip-location-db (https://github.com/sapics/ip-location-db)
    # Free, no API key required, daily updates, CC0/PDDL licensed
    provider: "ip-location-db"
    databases:
      asn:
        enabled: true
        url: "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@ip-location-db/asn-mmdb/asn.mmdb"
        file: "asn.mmdb"
      country:
        enabled: true
        url: "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@ip-location-db/geo-whois-asn-country-mmdb/geo-whois-asn-country.mmdb"
        file: "country.mmdb"
      city:
        enabled: true
        url: "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@ip-location-db/dbip-city-mmdb/dbip-city-ipv4.mmdb"
        file: "city.mmdb"
      whois:
        enabled: true
        url: "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@ip-location-db/geo-whois-asn-country-mmdb/geo-whois-asn-country.mmdb"
        file: "whois.mmdb"

  # Blocklists: stored in {data_dir}/security/blocklists/
  # Config is under server.security.blocklists (see Blocklists Section)

  cve:
    # NVD (NIST National Vulnerability Database)
    source: "https://nvd.nist.gov/feeds/json/cve/1.1"
    # Only download CVEs relevant to project dependencies
    filter_by_cpe: true

  trivy:
    # Aqua Trivy vulnerability database (optional, for container scanning)
    enabled: false
    source: "https://ghcr.io/aquasecurity/trivy-db"
```

### Security Directory Structure

```
{data_dir}/security/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ geoip/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ asn.mmdb                 # ASN lookups (AS number, organization)
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ country.mmdb             # Country code lookups
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ city.mmdb                # City, country, state, postcode, coordinates, timezone
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ whois.mmdb               # WHOIS data (registrant, org)
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ .last_updated            # Timestamp file
в”ңв”Җв”Җ blocklists/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ firehol_level1.txt
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ spamhaus_drop.txt
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ .last_updated
в”ңв”Җв”Җ cve/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ nvd.json
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ .last_updated
в””в”Җв”Җ trivy/
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ db.tar.gz
    в””в”Җв”Җ .last_updated
```

### GeoIP Database Details (ip-location-db)

| Database | File | Contains | Use Case |
|----------|------|----------|----------|
| ASN | `asn.mmdb` | AS number, AS organization | Network provider identification |
| Country | `country.mmdb` | Country code (ISO 3166-1) | Geo-blocking, compliance |
| City | `city.mmdb` | City, country, state1/state2, postcode, lat/lon, timezone | Location-based features |
| WHOIS | `whois.mmdb` | Registrant info, combined with ASN | Abuse detection, attribution |

**Benefits of ip-location-db:**
- No API key or account required (unlike MaxMind)
- Daily updates via jsDelivr CDN
- CC0/PDDL licensed (no restrictions)
- MMDB format (compatible with maxminddb crate)
- IPv4 and IPv6 support

## Display Environment Detection

**ALL binaries (server, CLI, agent) MUST detect display environment and adapt output accordingly.**

### Display Mode Hierarchy

| Mode | When Used | Requirements |
|------|-----------|--------------|
| **GUI** | Native display available, CLI binary only | X11, Wayland, Windows, macOS |
| **TUI** | Terminal available, interactive | TTY, SSH, mosh, screen, tmux |
| **CLI** | Command provided or piped output | Any environment |
| **Headless** | No display, no TTY | Daemon, service, cron |

### Platform Detection

| Platform | Display Check | Notes |
|----------|---------------|-------|
| **Linux/BSD** | `WAYLAND_DISPLAY` or `DISPLAY` | Wayland preferred over X11 |
| **macOS** | Always (unless SSH) | Native Cocoa display |
| **Windows** | Always (unless service) | Native Win32 display |
| **SSH/Mosh** | `SSH_CLIENT`, `SSH_TTY`, `MOSH` | No GUI, TUI or CLI only |

### Display Environment Detection

```rust
// src/common/display/detect.rs

use std::env;

/// UI display mode вҖ” distinct from application (AppMode)
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum DisplayMode {
    /// No display and no TTY (daemon/cron)
    Headless,
    /// Command-line only (piped or explicit command)
    Cli,
    /// Interactive terminal UI
    Tui,
    /// Native graphical UI
    Gui,
}

/// Detected display environment
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct DisplayEnv {
    pub mode: DisplayMode,
    /// X11, Wayland, Windows, or macOS display available
    pub has_display: bool,
    /// "x11", "wayland", "windows", "macos", or "none"
    pub display_type: String,
    /// stdout is a TTY
    pub is_terminal: bool,
    pub is_ssh: bool,
    pub is_mosh: bool,
    /// Running inside screen or tmux
    pub is_screen: bool,
    pub terminal_type: String,
    /// Terminal columns (0 if no terminal)
    pub cols: u16,
    /// Terminal rows (0 if no terminal)
    pub rows: u16,
}

impl DisplayEnv {
    /// Auto-detect display environment from the current process
    pub fn detect() -> Self {
        let terminal_type = env::var("TERM").unwrap_or_default();
        let is_terminal = atty::is(atty::Stream::Stdout);

        let (cols, rows) = if is_terminal {
            terminal_size::terminal_size()
                .map(|(w, h)| (w.0, h.0))
                .unwrap_or((0, 0))
        } else {
            (0, 0)
        };

        let is_ssh = env::var("SSH_CLIENT").is_ok() || env::var("SSH_TTY").is_ok();
        let is_mosh = env::var("MOSH").is_ok()
            || terminal_type.contains("mosh");
        let is_screen = env::var("STY").is_ok() || env::var("TMUX").is_ok();

        let (has_display, display_type) = detect_platform_display(is_ssh);

        let mode = auto_detect_mode(is_terminal, has_display, is_ssh, is_mosh, &terminal_type);

        Self {
            mode,
            has_display,
            display_type,
            is_terminal,
            is_ssh,
            is_mosh,
            is_screen,
            terminal_type,
            cols,
            rows,
        }
    }

    pub fn is_dumb_terminal(&self) -> bool {
        self.terminal_type == "dumb"
    }

    pub fn is_gui(&self) -> bool { self.mode == DisplayMode::Gui }
    pub fn is_tui(&self) -> bool { self.mode == DisplayMode::Tui }
    pub fn is_cli(&self) -> bool { self.mode == DisplayMode::Cli }
    pub fn is_headless(&self) -> bool { self.mode == DisplayMode::Headless }
}

fn auto_detect_mode(
    is_terminal: bool,
    has_display: bool,
    is_ssh: bool,
    is_mosh: bool,
    term: &str,
) -> DisplayMode {
    if !is_terminal && !has_display {
        return DisplayMode::Headless;
    }
    if term == "dumb" {
        return DisplayMode::Cli;
    }
    if has_display && !is_ssh && !is_mosh {
        return DisplayMode::Gui;
    }
    if is_terminal {
        return DisplayMode::Tui;
    }
    DisplayMode::Cli
}
```

### TERM=dumb Handling

**When `TERM=dumb`, the terminal has NO special capabilities. ALL binaries MUST handle this.**

| Feature | Normal Terminal | TERM=dumb |
|---------|-----------------|-----------|
| ANSI colors | Yes | No |
| Emojis | Yes | No |
| Cursor movement | Yes | No |
| Clear screen | Yes | No |
| Bold/underline | Yes | No |
| TUI mode | Yes | No (force CLI) |
| Spinners/progress | Yes | No (use text) |
| Box drawing (Unicode) | Yes | Maybe (ASCII fallback) |

**Behaviors when `TERM=dumb`:**

1. **Force CLI mode** - Never launch TUI, even if interactive
2. **No ANSI escapes** - No colors, no cursor control, no clearing
3. **No emojis** - Use text equivalents (`[OK]`, `[ERROR]`, `[WARN]`)
4. **Simple output** - Plain text, line-by-line
5. **No spinners** - Use `Processing...` / `Done.` instead
6. **No progress bars** - Use `50% complete` text instead
7. **ASCII tables** - Use `+--+` instead of `в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”җ` box drawing

**Implementation:**

```rust
/// Return true if ANSI escape sequences are safe to use
pub fn can_use_ansi(env: &DisplayEnv) -> bool {
    if env.is_dumb_terminal() {
        return false;
    }
    if std::env::var("NO_COLOR").map(|v| !v.is_empty()).unwrap_or(false) {
        return false;
    }
    env.is_terminal
}

/// Return a spinner appropriate for the terminal capability
pub fn new_spinner(env: &DisplayEnv, message: &str) -> Box<dyn Spinner> {
    if env.is_dumb_terminal() {
        Box::new(TextSpinner::new(message))
    } else {
        Box::new(AnsiSpinner::new(message))
    }
}

/// Print a progress indicator appropriate for the terminal capability
pub fn show_progress(env: &DisplayEnv, percent: u8) {
    if env.is_dumb_terminal() {
        println!("{percent}% complete");
        return;
    }
    let filled = (percent as usize) / 2;
    print!("\r[{:=<50}] {percent}%", "=".repeat(filled));
}
```

**Testing:**

```bash
# Test dumb terminal behavior
TERM=dumb {project_name} --status
TERM=dumb {project_name}-cli list
TERM=dumb {project_name}-agent status

# Should produce plain text output with no escape codes
TERM=dumb {project_name} --status | cat -v   # No ^[ sequences
```

### Platform-Specific Display Detection

```rust
// src/common/display/detect_unix.rs
// Compiled on Linux/BSD/macOS (cfg not windows)

#[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
pub fn detect_platform_display(is_ssh: bool) -> (bool, String) {
    use std::env;

    if let Ok(wd) = env::var("WAYLAND_DISPLAY") {
        if !wd.is_empty() {
            return (true, "wayland".into());
        }
    }

    if let Ok(d) = env::var("DISPLAY") {
        if !d.is_empty() {
            return (true, "x11".into());
        }
    }

    #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
    {
        if !is_ssh {
            if env::var("__CFBundleIdentifier").is_ok() {
                return (true, "macos".into());
            }
            let output = std::process::Command::new("launchctl")
                .arg("managername")
                .output();
            if let Ok(out) = output {
                if String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stdout).contains("Aqua") {
                    return (true, "macos".into());
                }
            }
        }
    }

    (false, "none".into())
}
```

```rust
// src/common/display/detect_windows.rs

#[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
pub fn detect_platform_display(_is_ssh: bool) -> (bool, String) {
    use std::env;
    use windows::Win32::System::Threading::GetCurrentProcessId;
    use windows::Win32::System::RemoteDesktop::ProcessIdToSessionId;

    let pid = unsafe { GetCurrentProcessId() };
    let mut session_id: u32 = 0;
    unsafe { ProcessIdToSessionId(pid, &mut session_id) };

    if session_id == 0 {
        return (false, "none".into());
    }

    let session_name = env::var("SESSIONNAME").unwrap_or_default();
    if session_name.starts_with("RDP-Tcp") || !session_name.is_empty() {
        return (true, "windows-rdp".into());
    }

    (true, "windows".into())
}
```

### Binary-Specific Behavior

| Binary | GUI | TUI | CLI | Headless |
|--------|-----|-----|-----|----------|
| **Server** | Status window | Status banner | Commands | Default (daemon) |
| **CLI** | Full app | Full app (default) | Commands | Error |
| **Agent** | Status window | Status banner | Commands | Default (service) |

**Server/Agent just show status banners (not interactive). CLI is the only full TUI/GUI app with setup wizard.**

**See PART 33 for full CLI/TUI/GUI mode implementation details.**

### Display Package

**See "Display Environment Detection" section above for full `DisplayMode` and `DisplayEnv` definitions.**

### Terminal Package

```rust
// src/common/terminal/size.rs

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord)]
pub enum SizeMode {
    /// <40 cols or <10 rows
    Micro,
    /// 40-59 cols or 10-15 rows
    Minimal,
    /// 60-79 cols or 16-23 rows
    Compact,
    /// 80-119 cols and 24-39 rows
    Standard,
    /// 120-199 cols and 40-59 rows
    Wide,
    /// 200-399 cols and 60-79 rows
    Ultrawide,
    /// 400+ cols and 80+ rows
    Massive,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub struct TerminalSize {
    pub cols: u16,
    pub rows: u16,
    pub mode: SizeMode,
}

pub fn get_terminal_size() -> TerminalSize {
    let (cols, rows) = terminal_size::terminal_size()
        .map(|(w, h)| (w.0, h.0))
        .unwrap_or((80, 24));

    let cols = cols.max(1);
    let rows = rows.max(1);
    let mode = calculate_mode(cols, rows);

    TerminalSize { cols, rows, mode }
}

fn calculate_mode(cols: u16, rows: u16) -> SizeMode {
    match (cols, rows) {
        (c, r) if c < 40 || r < 10 => SizeMode::Micro,
        (c, r) if c < 60 || r < 16 => SizeMode::Minimal,
        (c, r) if c < 80 || r < 24 => SizeMode::Compact,
        (c, r) if c < 120 || r < 40 => SizeMode::Standard,
        (c, r) if c < 200 || r < 60 => SizeMode::Wide,
        (c, r) if c < 400 || r < 80 => SizeMode::Ultrawide,
        _ => SizeMode::Massive,
    }
}

impl SizeMode {
    pub fn show_ascii_art(self) -> bool { self >= SizeMode::Standard }
    pub fn show_borders(self) -> bool   { self >= SizeMode::Compact }
    pub fn show_sidebar(self) -> bool   { self >= SizeMode::Wide }
    pub fn show_icons(self) -> bool     { self >= SizeMode::Minimal }
}
```

### Theme Package

**See PART 16 "Unified Color Palette" for `ThemePalette` struct and color definitions.**

Usage in common/theme:
```rust
// src/common/theme/colors.rs

// ThemePalette, THEME_PALETTE_DARK, THEME_PALETTE_LIGHT вҖ” see PART 16

pub fn get_theme_palette(theme_mode: &str) -> &'static ThemePalette {
    match theme_mode {
        "light" => &THEME_PALETTE_LIGHT,
        "auto" => {
            if is_system_dark_theme() {
                &THEME_PALETTE_DARK
            } else {
                &THEME_PALETTE_LIGHT
            }
        }
        _ => &THEME_PALETTE_DARK,
    }
}
```

### Banner Package

```rust
// src/common/banner/banner.rs

use crate::common::terminal::{get_terminal_size, SizeMode};

pub struct BannerConfig {
    pub app_name: String,
    pub version: String,
    pub app_mode: String,
    pub debug: bool,
    pub urls: Vec<String>,
    /// Show setup token (server only, first run)
    pub show_setup: bool,
    pub setup_token: String,
}

pub fn print_startup_banner(cfg: &BannerConfig) {
    let size = get_terminal_size();
    match size.mode {
        m if m >= SizeMode::Standard => print_banner_full(cfg, &size),
        m if m >= SizeMode::Compact  => print_banner_compact(cfg),
        m if m >= SizeMode::Minimal  => print_banner_minimal(cfg),
        _                            => print_banner_micro(cfg),
    }
}
```

---


# PART 8: SERVER BINARY CLI

**These are the command-line flags for the SERVER binary (`{project_name}`), NOT the client (`{project_name}-cli`).**

## Binary Types

| Binary | Default Name | Purpose | Key Flags |
|--------|--------------|---------|-----------|
| **Server** | `{project_name}` | Runs the HTTP server | `--config`, `--data`, `--port`, `--mode` |
| **Agent** | `{project_name}-agent` | Reports to server | `--server`, `--token`, `--config` |
| **Client** | `{project_name}-cli` | User interface to server | `--server`, `--token`, `--output` |

**Shared flags (ALL binaries):** `--help`, `--version`, `--shell`, `--debug`, `--color`, `--lang`

**Binary naming rules (ALL binaries: server, agent, client):**

| Binary | Default Name | User-Agent |
|--------|--------------|------------|
| Server | `{project_name}` | `{project_name}/{version}` |
| Agent | `{project_name}-agent` | `{project_name}-agent/{version}` |
| Client | `{project_name}-cli` | `{project_name}-cli/{version}` |

**ALL binaries can be renamed by users. Must show ACTUAL binary name in:**
- `--help` and `--version` output
- Error messages showing "run X --help"
- Any user-facing documentation/instructions

**Hardcode `{project_name}` for internal identifiers (never changes):**
- User-Agent header (identifies binary type to server)
- Default paths (`/etc/{project_org}/{internal_name}/`)
- Config keys, database tables, API identifiers

**Get actual binary name:**
```rust
let binary_name = std::env::args()
    .next()
    .as_deref()
    .and_then(|p| std::path::Path::new(p).file_name())
    .and_then(|n| n.to_str())
    .unwrap_or("{project_name}")
    .to_string();
// Use binary_name for display; use hardcoded project name for User-Agent
```

**Example (user renames `jokes` to `myapp`):**
```
$ myapp --help
Usage: myapp [options]              # Shows actual name

$ myapp --version
myapp 1.0.0 (abc123)                # Shows actual name

# But internally:
User-Agent: jokes/1.0.0             # Hardcoded project name
Default config: /etc/apimgr/jokes/  # Hardcoded project name
```

**For client and agent flags, see PART 33.**

## NO_COLOR Support (ALL Binaries)

**All binaries (server, client, agent) MUST respect the [NO_COLOR](https://no-color.org/) standard.**

When the `NO_COLOR` environment variable is set and not empty (value doesn't matter), disable ANSI color codes and emojis in terminal output.

| Condition | Colors | Emojis |
|-----------|--------|--------|
| `NO_COLOR` unset | Enabled | Enabled |
| `NO_COLOR=""` (empty) | Enabled | Enabled |
| `NO_COLOR=1` | **Disabled** | **Disabled** |
| `NO_COLOR=anything` | **Disabled** | **Disabled** |

**Priority order (highest to lowest):**

| Priority | Source | Example |
|----------|--------|---------|
| 1 | CLI flag | `--color=yes`, `--color=no` |
| 2 | Config file | `output.color: false` |
| 3 | `NO_COLOR` env var | `NO_COLOR=1` |
| 4 | Auto-detect | TTY check, `TERM` variable |

**Implementation (ALL binaries):**

```rust
/// Return true if color output should be used
pub fn color_enabled(force_color: Option<bool>) -> bool {
    // 1. CLI flag overrides everything
    if let Some(forced) = force_color {
        return forced;
    }

    // 2. Config file (if applicable)
    if let Some(cfg) = get_config() {
        if cfg.output.color_set {
            return cfg.output.color;
        }
    }

    // 3. NO_COLOR env var (non-empty = disable)
    if std::env::var("NO_COLOR").map(|v| !v.is_empty()).unwrap_or(false) {
        return false;
    }

    // 4. Auto-detect: TTY + TERM support
    if !atty::is(atty::Stream::Stdout) {
        return false;
    }
    if std::env::var("TERM").as_deref() == Ok("dumb") {
        return false;
    }

    true
}

/// Return true if emoji output should be used
pub fn emoji_enabled() -> bool {
    // 1. Config override (force emojis on)
    if let Some(cfg) = get_config() {
        if cfg.output.emoji_set && cfg.output.emoji {
            return true;
        }
    }    // 2. NO_COLOR disables emojis (practical plain output)
    if std::env::var("NO_COLOR").is_ok() {
        return false;
    }

    // 3. TERM=dumb disables emojis
    if std::env::var("TERM").unwrap_or_default() == "dumb" {
        return false;
    }

    // 4. Default: enabled
    true
}
```

**Scope:** NO_COLOR disables:
- ANSI color codes
- Emojis in terminal output (practical: users wanting plain output)

**NO_COLOR does NOT affect:**
- Bold/underline/italic text styling
- Unicode box drawing characters (tables)
- Output structure or formatting

**Config override:** To keep emojis enabled even when NO_COLOR is set:
```yaml
output:
  emoji: true  # Force emojis on (overrides NO_COLOR for emojis only)
```

**Note:** For disabling ALL ANSI escapes (not just colors), use `TERM=dumb`. See PART 7 "TERM=dumb Handling".

**Testing:**
```bash
# Colors and emojis enabled (default)
{project_name} --status

# Colors and emojis disabled via NO_COLOR
NO_COLOR=1 {project_name} --status

# Colors forced on (overrides NO_COLOR for colors only)
NO_COLOR=1 {project_name} --status --color=yes

# Colors forced off (explicit)
{project_name} --status --color=no

# Verify no escape codes or emojis in output
NO_COLOR=1 {project_name} --status | cat -v   # No ^[ sequences
NO_COLOR=1 {project_name} --status | grep -E 'вң…|вқҢ|вҡ пёҸ|рҹҡҖ'  # Should find nothing
```

**THESE SERVER COMMANDS CANNOT BE CHANGED. This is the complete command set.**

## Server Binary Commands

```bash
--help                       # Show help (can be run by anyone)
--version                    # Show version (can be run by anyone)
--shell completions [SHELL]  # Print shell completions (auto-detect if SHELL omitted)
--shell init [SHELL]         # Print shell init for eval (auto-detect if SHELL omitted)
--mode {production|development}  # Set application mode
--config {config_dir}         # Set config directory
--data {data_dir}             # Set data directory
--cache {cache_dir}           # Set cache directory
--log {log_dir}               # Set log directory
--backup {backup_dir}         # Set backup directory
--pid {pid_file}              # Set PID file path
--address {listen}           # Set listen address
--port {port}                # Set the port
--baseurl {path}             # Set URL path prefix (default: /)
--status                     # Show status and health (exit 0=healthy, 1=unhealthy)
--service {start,restart,stop,reload,--install,--uninstall,--disable,--help}
--daemon                     # Daemonize (detach from terminal)
--debug                      # Enable debug mode (verbose logging, debug endpoints)
--color {auto|yes|no}        # Color output (default: auto, respects NO_COLOR)
--lang {code}                # Language for output (default: auto, from LANG env)
--maintenance {backup,restore,update,mode,setup,--help} [optional-file-or-setting]
--update [check|yes|branch {stable|beta|daily}|--help]  # Check/perform updates
--shell {completions,init,--help} [SHELL]  # Shell integration
```

### Server --help Output

```bash
$ {project_name} --help
{project_name} {projectversion} - {project description}

Usage:
  {project_name} [flags]

Information:
-h, --help                             - Show help (--help for any command shows its help)
-v, --version                          - Show version
--status                               - Show server status and health

Shell Integration:
--shell completions [SHELL]            - Print shell completions
--shell init [SHELL]                   - Print shell init command
--shell help                           - Show shell help

Server Configuration:
--mode {production|development}        - Application mode (default: production)
--config DIR                           - Config directory
--data DIR                             - Data directory
--cache DIR                            - Cache directory
--log DIR                              - Log directory
--backup DIR                           - Backup directory
--pid FILE                             - PID file path
--address ADDR                         - Listen address (default: 0.0.0.0)
--port PORT                            - Listen port (default: random 64xxx, 80 in container)
--baseurl PATH                         - URL path prefix (default: /)
--daemon                               - Run as daemon (detach from terminal)
--debug                                - Enable debug mode
--color {auto|yes|no}                  - Color output (default: auto)
--lang CODE                            - Language for output (default: auto)

Service Management:
--service CMD                          - Service management (run --service help for details)
--maintenance CMD                      - Maintenance operations (run --maintenance help for details)
--update [CMD]                         - Check/perform updates (run --update help for details)

Run '{project_name} <command> help' for detailed help on any command.
```

## Directory Flags

**All directory flags MUST create directories if they don't exist.**

| Flag | Type | Default (Linux root) | Default (Linux user) |
|------|------|----------------------|----------------------|
| `--config` | Directory | `/etc/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` | `~/.config/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` |
| `--data` | Directory | `/var/lib/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` | `~/.local/share/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` |
| `--cache` | Directory | `/var/cache/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` | `~/.cache/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` |
| `--log` | Directory | `/var/log/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` | `~/.local/log/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` |
| `--backup` | Directory | `/mnt/Backups/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` (if writable) | `~/.local/share/Backups/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` |
| `--pid` | File | `/var/run/{project_org}/{internal_name}.pid` | `~/.local/share/{project_org}/{internal_name}/{internal_name}.pid` |

**Note:** `--backup` prefers system backup dir if writable, falls back to user dir. See `get_backup_dir()` in PART 5.

### Directory Validation Rules

| Rule | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Create if missing** | All directories are created with proper permissions if they don't exist |
| **Permissions (root)** | Directories: `0755`, Files: `0644`, PID: `0644` |
| **Permissions (user)** | Directories: `0700`, Files: `0600`, PID: `0600` |
| **Parent dirs** | Create parent directories as needed (`fs::create_dir_all` equivalent) |
| **Validate writable** | Fail fast if directory is not writable |
| **Log on create** | Log directory creation at INFO level |

### Implementation

```rust
use std::fs;
use std::io::Write;
use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;
use std::path::Path;
use anyhow::{Context, Result};

// ensure_dir creates directory with proper permissions if it doesn't exist
pub fn ensure_dir(path: &Path, is_root: bool) -> Result<()> {
    let perm_mode = if is_root { 0o755 } else { 0o700 };

    fs::create_dir_all(path)
        .with_context(|| format!("failed to create directory {}", path.display()))?;

    fs::set_permissions(path, fs::Permissions::from_mode(perm_mode))
        .with_context(|| format!("failed to set permissions on {}", path.display()))?;

    // Verify writable
    let test_file = path.join(".write-test");
    fs::write(&test_file, b"")
        .with_context(|| format!("directory {} is not writable", path.display()))?;
    let _ = fs::remove_file(&test_file);

    Ok(())
}

// ensure_pid_file creates PID file directory and validates path
pub fn ensure_pid_file(path: &Path, is_root: bool) -> Result<()> {
    let dir = path.parent().unwrap_or(Path::new("."));
    ensure_dir(dir, is_root)
}
```

### PID File Handling

**Stale PID detection is REQUIRED.** A crash or kill -9 leaves stale PID files.

```rust
use std::fs;
use std::path::Path;
use anyhow::Result;

// check_pid_file checks if PID file exists and if the process is still running.
// Returns (is_running, pid).
pub fn check_pid_file(pid_path: &Path) -> Result<(bool, u32)> {
    let data = match fs::read_to_string(pid_path) {
        Ok(d) => d,
        Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => {
            // No PID file, not running
            return Ok((false, 0));
        }
        Err(e) => return Err(e.into()),
    };

    let pid: u32 = match data.trim().parse() {
        Ok(p) => p,
        Err(_) => {
            // Corrupt PID file - remove it
            let _ = fs::remove_file(pid_path);
            return Ok((false, 0));
        }
    };

    if !is_process_running(pid) {
        // Stale PID file - remove it
        let _ = fs::remove_file(pid_path);
        return Ok((false, 0));
    }

    if !is_our_process(pid) {
        // PID was reused by another process - remove stale file
        let _ = fs::remove_file(pid_path);
        return Ok((false, 0));
    }

    Ok((true, pid))
}

// Platform-specific process checks вҖ” see src/pid/pid_unix.rs and pid_windows.rs

// --- src/pid/pid_unix.rs ---
// #[cfg(unix)]

// is_process_running checks if a process with given PID exists (Unix)
#[cfg(unix)]
pub fn is_process_running(pid: u32) -> bool {
    // send signal 0 to check existence without delivering a signal
    unsafe { libc::kill(pid as libc::pid_t, 0) == 0 }
}

// is_our_process verifies the process is actually our binary (Unix)
#[cfg(unix)]
pub fn is_our_process(pid: u32) -> bool {
    // Linux: read /proc/{pid}/exe symlink
    let exe_link = format!("/proc/{}/exe", pid);
    if let Ok(exe_path) = std::fs::read_link(&exe_link) {
        return exe_path
            .file_name()
            .and_then(|n| n.to_str())
            .map(|n| n.contains("{project_name}"))
            .unwrap_or(false);
    }
    // macOS/BSD: fall back to ps
    is_our_process_darwin(pid)
}

#[cfg(unix)]
fn is_our_process_darwin(pid: u32) -> bool {
    let output = std::process::Command::new("ps")
        .args(["-p", &pid.to_string(), "-o", "comm="])
        .output();
    match output {
        Ok(o) => String::from_utf8_lossy(&o.stdout).contains("{project_name}"),
        Err(_) => false,
    }
}

// --- src/pid/pid_windows.rs ---
// #[cfg(windows)]

// is_process_running checks if a process with given PID exists (Windows)
#[cfg(windows)]
pub fn is_process_running(pid: u32) -> bool {
    use windows::Win32::System::Threading::{
        OpenProcess, PROCESS_QUERY_LIMITED_INFORMATION,
    };
    use windows::Win32::Foundation::CloseHandle;
    unsafe {
        match OpenProcess(PROCESS_QUERY_LIMITED_INFORMATION, false, pid) {
            Ok(handle) => {
                let _ = CloseHandle(handle);
                true
            }
            Err(_) => false,
        }
    }
}

// is_our_process verifies the process is actually our binary (Windows)
#[cfg(windows)]
pub fn is_our_process(pid: u32) -> bool {
    use windows::Win32::System::Threading::{
        OpenProcess, QueryFullProcessImageNameW, PROCESS_QUERY_LIMITED_INFORMATION,
        PROCESS_NAME_WIN32,
    };
    use windows::Win32::Foundation::CloseHandle;
    use windows::core::PWSTR;
    unsafe {
        let Ok(handle) = OpenProcess(PROCESS_QUERY_LIMITED_INFORMATION, false, pid) else {
            return false;
        };
        let mut buf = vec![0u16; 260];
        let mut size = buf.len() as u32;
        let ok = QueryFullProcessImageNameW(
            handle,
            PROCESS_NAME_WIN32,
            PWSTR(buf.as_mut_ptr()),
            &mut size,
        )
        .is_ok();
        let _ = CloseHandle(handle);
        if !ok {
            return false;
        }
        let name = String::from_utf16_lossy(&buf[..size as usize]);
        std::path::Path::new(&name)
            .file_name()
            .and_then(|n| n.to_str())
            .map(|n| n.to_lowercase().contains("{project_name}"))
            .unwrap_or(false)
    }
}

// write_pid_file writes current process PID to file
pub fn write_pid_file(pid_path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    let (running, existing_pid) = check_pid_file(pid_path)?;
    if running {
        anyhow::bail!("already running (pid {})", existing_pid);
    }
    let pid = std::process::id();
    fs::write(pid_path, pid.to_string())?;
    Ok(())
}

// remove_pid_file removes PID file on shutdown
pub fn remove_pid_file(pid_path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
    fs::remove_file(pid_path)?;
    Ok(())
}
```

**Startup Flow:**

```
1. Check if PID file exists
   в”ңв”Җв–ә No  вҶ’ Create PID file, start server
   в””в”Җв–ә Yes вҶ’ Read PID from file
             в”ңв”Җв–ә Process running AND is our binary вҶ’ Exit "already running"
             в”ңв”Җв–ә Process running BUT different binary вҶ’ Remove stale, start
             в””в”Җв–ә Process not running вҶ’ Remove stale, start
```

**Shutdown Flow:**

```
1. Receive SIGTERM/SIGINT
2. Graceful shutdown (close connections, flush logs)
3. Remove PID file
4. Exit
```

**Important:**
- Always remove PID file in signal handlers
- Use drop guards + signal handler for cleanup
- Check for stale PIDs on EVERY startup
- Verify process identity to handle PID reuse

### Server Startup Sequence

**The server binary handles ALL initialization. No external scripts needed.**

```
PHASE 1: Parse and handle immediate-exit flags (NO startup)
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
1. Parse CLI flags and environment variables
2. Handle immediate-exit flags вҶ’ print output and EXIT:
   в”ңв”Җ --help                      вҶ’ print main help, exit 0
   в”ңв”Җ --version                   вҶ’ print version, exit 0
   в”ңв”Җ --status                    вҶ’ query running server, print status, exit 0/1
   в”ңв”Җ --shell --help              вҶ’ print shell help, exit 0
   в”ңв”Җ --shell completions         вҶ’ print completions, exit 0
   в”ңв”Җ --shell init                вҶ’ print init command, exit 0
   в”ңв”Җ --service --help            вҶ’ print service help, exit 0
   в”ңв”Җ --maintenance --help        вҶ’ print maintenance help, exit 0
   в””в”Җ --update --help             вҶ’ print update help, exit 0

PHASE 2: Handle service commands (NO startup)
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
3. Handle --service subcommands вҶ’ execute and EXIT:
   в”ңв”Җ --service start     вҶ’ start via init system, exit
   в”ңв”Җ --service stop      вҶ’ stop via init system, exit
   в”ңв”Җ --service restart   вҶ’ restart via init system, exit
   в”ңв”Җ --service reload    вҶ’ reload via init system, exit
   в”ңв”Җ --service --install вҶ’ install service file, enable, start, exit
   в”ңв”Җ --service --disable вҶ’ stop, disable, exit
   в””в”Җ --service --uninstall вҶ’ stop, disable, remove all, exit

PHASE 3: Handle maintenance commands (NO startup)
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
4. Handle --maintenance subcommands вҶ’ execute and EXIT:
   в”ңв”Җ --maintenance backup  вҶ’ backup data, exit
   в”ңв”Җ --maintenance restore вҶ’ restore data, exit
   в”ңв”Җ --maintenance update  вҶ’ update binary, exit
   в”ңв”Җ --maintenance mode    вҶ’ set mode, exit
   в””в”Җ --maintenance setup   вҶ’ run setup wizard, exit

PHASE 4: Handle update commands (NO startup)
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
5. Handle --update subcommands вҶ’ execute and EXIT:
   в”ңв”Җ --update check        вҶ’ check for updates, exit
   в”ңв”Җ --update yes          вҶ’ download/install update, exit
   в””в”Җ --update branch <name> вҶ’ switch branch, exit

PHASE 5: Server startup (actual server start)
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
6. Determine run context:
   в”ңв”Җ Root vs user (UID == 0?)
   в”ңв”Җ Container vs local (/.dockerenv exists? container env var?)
   в””в”Җ Detect service manager (systemd, launchd, runit, etc.)

7. Resolve all paths (CLI flags вҶ’ env vars вҶ’ context-based defaults):
   в”ңв”Җ {config_dir}  (/etc/... or ~/.config/...)
   в”ңв”Җ {data_dir}    (/var/lib/... or ~/.local/share/...)
   в”ңв”Җ {cache_dir}   (/var/cache/... or ~/.cache/...)
   в”ңв”Җ {log_dir}     (/var/log/... or ~/.local/log/...)
   в””в”Җ {backup_dir}  (see PART 5 get_backup_dir - /mnt/Backups/... if writable)

8. IF RUNNING AS ROOT - setup system resources BEFORE dropping privileges:
   a. Check/create system user:
      в”ңв”Җ User {project_name} exists вҶ’ use it
      в””в”Җ User missing вҶ’ create {internal_name}:{internal_name} (see PART 25)
   b. Create ALL directories (while still root):
      в”ңв”Җ {config_dir}/ and subdirs (ssl/, tor/)
      в”ңв”Җ {data_dir}/ and subdirs (db/, security/, tor/, tor/site/)
      в”ңв”Җ {cache_dir}/
      в”ңв”Җ {log_dir}/
      в””в”Җ {backup_dir}/
   c. Set ownership: chown -R {internal_name}:{internal_name} on all dirs
   d. Set permissions: 0755 general dirs, 0700 sensitive (security/, ssl/, tor/)
   e. Determine ports (see PART 15 for full port rules):
      в”ңв”Җ Format 1: --port {port} (single port)
      в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ HTTP by default
      в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ If port is 443 вҶ’ HTTPS-only mode
      в”Ӯ   в””в”Җ If ssl.enabled in config вҶ’ HTTPS on that port
      в”ңв”Җ Format 2: --port {http},{https} (dual port)
      в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ First port = HTTP
      в”Ӯ   в””в”Җ Second port = HTTPS
      в”ңв”Җ No --port? вҶ’ check {PROJECT_NAME}_PORT env var (same format)
      в”ңв”Җ No env var? вҶ’ read from config file (server.port)
      в””в”Җ No config? вҶ’ random port in 64000-64999 range (single, HTTP)
   f. Bind ALL privileged ports (< 1024) NOW while still root:
      в”ңв”Җ For each port < 1024: create and bind socket, store fd
      в”ңв”Җ If ANY privileged port fails: exit with error
      в””в”Җ Unprivileged ports (>= 1024) bound later in step 18
   g. DROP PRIVILEGES to {project_name} user
   h. Verify privilege drop succeeded (getuid() != 0)

9. IF RUNNING AS USER (non-root) - setup user directories:
   в”ңв”Җ Create {config_dir} (~/.config/{project_org}/{internal_name}/)
   в”ңв”Җ Create {data_dir} (~/.local/share/{project_org}/{internal_name}/)
   в”ңв”Җ Create {cache_dir} (~/.cache/{project_org}/{internal_name}/)
   в”ңв”Җ Create {log_dir} (~/.local/log/{project_org}/{internal_name}/)
   в”ңв”Җ Create {backup_dir} (~/.local/share/Backups/{project_org}/{internal_name}/)
   в”ңв”Җ Set permissions: 0700 on all dirs (user-only access)
   в””в”Җ Note: port must be >1024 (user mode cannot bind privileged ports)

10. Initialize logging:
    в”ңв”Җ Open {log_dir}/server.log for writing
    в”ңв”Җ Set default log level (info)
    в””в”Җ Log "Server starting, version X.Y.Z"

11. Check PID file (root: /var/run/{project_org}/{internal_name}.pid, user: {data_dir}/{internal_name}.pid):
    в”ңв”Җ PID file exists AND process running вҶ’ exit 1 "already running"
    в”ңв”Җ PID file exists AND process dead вҶ’ remove stale PID, continue
    в””в”Җ No PID file вҶ’ continue

12. Write PID file (current process ID)

13. Load configuration:
    в”ңв”Җ Look for {config_dir}/server.yml
    в”ңв”Җ Determine port (if not already bound in step 8f):
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ --port CLI flag вҶ’ use specified
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ {PROJECT_NAME}_PORT env var вҶ’ use specified
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ Config file server.port вҶ’ use specified
    в”Ӯ   в””в”Җ Default вҶ’ random 64000-64999
    в”ңв”Җ If MISSING (first run):
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ Generate default server.yml
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ Save determined port(s) to config
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ Generate one-time setup token (32 hex chars)
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ Write config file
    в”Ӯ   в””в”Җ Set first_run = true
    в”ңв”Җ If EXISTS:
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ Parse YAML configuration
    в”Ӯ   в””в”Җ Validate all values (invalid вҶ’ log WARN, use default)
    в””в”Җ Apply remaining environment variable overrides ({PROJECT_NAME}_*)

14. Reconfigure logging from config:
    в”ңв”Җ Set log level from server.logging.level
    в”ңв”Җ Set log format from server.logging.format
    в””в”Җ Configure rotation (max_size, max_files)

15. Initialize database:
    в”ңв”Җ Connect to database (SQLite: {data_dir}/db/server.db)
    в”ңв”Җ Create database file if missing
    в”ңв”Җ Run CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for all tables
    в”ңв”Җ Run schema migrations if needed
    в””в”Җ Verify database integrity

16. Start scheduler (see PART 19 for full task list):
    в”ңв”Җ Initialize background task scheduler
    в”ңв”Җ Load task state from database
    в”ңв”Җ Register built-in tasks (key tasks):
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ backup_daily (02:00 daily)
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ ssl_renewal (03:00 daily)
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ session_cleanup (every 15m)
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ geoip_update (03:00 Sunday)
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ public_ip_refresh (startup + every 12h, hardcoded)
    в”Ӯ   в””в”Җ ... and others (see PART 19)
    в””в”Җ Start scheduler task (tokio::spawn)

17. Start Tor (if tor binary available) - see PART 32:
    в”ңв”Җ tor not found in PATH вҶ’ log INFO "Tor not available", skip
    в”ңв”Җ tor found:
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ Create directories: {config_dir}/tor/, {data_dir}/tor/, {data_dir}/tor/site/
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ Set permissions: 0700 for dirs, 0600 for files
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ Generate and write {config_dir}/tor/torrc
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ Start dedicated tor process (TorrcFile + DataDir)
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ Control: localhost TCP via 127.0.0.1:auto
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ Wait for bootstrap (up to 3 min)
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ Create hidden service, log .onion address
    в”Ӯ   в””в”Җ Error вҶ’ log WARN, continue without Tor (non-fatal)

18. Start HTTP server:
    в”ңв”Җ For each configured port:
    в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җ IF port < 1024: use pre-bound socket from step 8f
    в”Ӯ   в””в”Җ IF port >= 1024: bind socket now (as unprivileged user)
    в”ңв”Җ Configure TLS on ports that need it (typically 443)
    в”ңв”Җ Register all route handlers
    в””в”Җ Begin accepting connections on all ports

19. Register signal handlers (see PART 8 for full signal table):
    в”ңв”Җ SIGTERM вҶ’ graceful shutdown
    в”ңв”Җ SIGINT  вҶ’ graceful shutdown
    в”ңв”Җ SIGQUIT вҶ’ graceful shutdown
    в”ңв”Җ SIGHUP  вҶ’ ignored (config auto-reloads via file watcher)
    в”ңв”Җ SIGUSR1 вҶ’ reopen log files (for rotation)
    в””в”Җ SIGUSR2 вҶ’ dump status to log

20. Log startup complete:
    в”ңв”Җ Log "Listening on {address}:{port}"
    в”ңв”Җ Log "Mode: {production|development}"
    в”ңв”Җ Log "Tor: {.onion address}" (if enabled)
    в””в”Җ If first_run: display setup token in console

21. Enter main loop (block until shutdown signal received)
```

| Step | Runs As | Why |
|------|---------|-----|
| 6. Determine context | any | First thing - detect if root, container, etc. |
| 7. Resolve paths | any | Path resolution based on context |
| **IF ROOT (step 8):** | | |
| 8a. Create system user | **root** | Only root can create system users |
| 8b. Create directories | **root** | Only root can create /etc/, /var/lib/, etc. |
| 8c. Set ownership (chown) | **root** | Only root can chown to another user |
| 8d. Set permissions | **root** | Easier while root, ensures correct perms |
| 8e. Determine ports | **root** | Need to know before binding |
| 8f. Bind privileged ports | **root** | Only root can bind port < 1024 |
| **8g. DROP PRIVILEGES** | **rootвҶ’user** | Security: minimize time running as root |
| **IF USER (step 9):** | | |
| 9. Setup user directories | **user** | Create ~/.config/, ~/.local/share/, etc. |
| **COMMON PATH:** | | |
| 10-21. Everything else | **user** | Dirs exist, privileged sockets bound (if any) |

**Security principle:** Drop privileges as EARLY as possible, but AFTER:
1. Creating directories and setting ownership
2. Binding any privileged ports (< 1024)

**What REQUIRES root (steps 8a-8f):**
- Creating system directories (/etc/, /var/lib/, /var/log/, /var/cache/, /var/backups/)
- Creating system user/group
- chown directories to app user
- Setting permissions on system directories
- Binding to privileged ports (80, 443, etc.)

**What does NOT require root (steps 10-21):**
- Writing files to directories owned by app user (logging, config, database, PID)
- Binding to unprivileged ports (>= 1024)
- Accepting connections on pre-bound privileged sockets
- Starting child processes (tor, scheduler)
- Signal handling

**Port binding examples (see PART 15 for full rules):**

| Config | Port(s) | Bind As | Protocol |
|--------|---------|---------|----------|
| `--port 8080` | 8080 | user | HTTP |
| `--port 443` | 443 | root | HTTPS |
| `--port 80,443` | 80, 443 | root (both) | HTTP, HTTPS |
| `--port 8080,8443` | 8080, 8443 | user (both) | HTTP, HTTPS |

**Key principle:** Drop the binary anywhere, run it, everything works. No setup scripts, no manual directory creation, no permission fixing.

### Daemonization (--daemon flag)

**By default, the server runs in foreground.** Modern service managers (systemd, launchd) prefer this.

**`--service start` ignores `daemonize` setting** - it auto-detects the service manager and does the right thing.

| Start Method | Daemonize Behavior |
|--------------|-------------------|
| `--service start` (systemd) | Always foreground (ignores config) |
| `--service start` (launchd) | Always foreground (ignores config) |
| `--service start` (runit/s6) | Always foreground (ignores config) |
| `--service start` (container) | Always foreground (ignores config) |
| `--service start` (SysV init) | Always daemonize (ignores config) |
| `--service start` (rc.d/BSD) | Always daemonize (ignores config) |
| Manual start (no --service) | Respects `--daemon` flag and config |

**Container Detection (always foreground):**

| Detection Method | Check |
|------------------|-------|
| Docker | `/.dockerenv` exists |
| Podman | `/run/.containerenv` exists |
| LXC/LXD/Incus | `/dev/lxc` exists OR `container=lxc` env |
| systemd-nspawn | `container=systemd-nspawn` env |
| Generic | `container` env var set (any value) |
| Init systems | Parent PID 1 is: `tini`, `dumb-init`, `s6-svscan`, `runsv`, `runsvdir`, `catatonit` |
| Kubernetes | `KUBERNETES_SERVICE_HOST` env var set |
| cgroup | `/proc/1/cgroup` contains `docker`, `kubepods`, `lxc` |
| Self wrapper | Parent process is `{project_name}` (entrypoint wrapper) |

**Manual Start Priority Order:**
1. `--daemon` CLI flag (highest)
2. `server.daemonize` config setting
3. Default: `false` (foreground)

| Flag/Config | Behavior |
|-------------|----------|
| (default) | Run in foreground |
| `--daemon` or `daemonize: true` | Fork, detach from terminal, run in background |

**Service Manager Detection:**

```rust
use std::fs;
use std::path::Path;

// is_container returns true if running inside a container
pub fn is_container() -> bool {
    // File-based detection
    let container_files = [
        "/.dockerenv",           // Docker
        "/run/.containerenv",    // Podman
        "/dev/lxc",              // LXC/LXD/Incus
    ];
    for f in &container_files {
        if Path::new(f).exists() {
            return true;
        }
    }

    // Environment variable detection
    if std::env::var("container").is_ok() {
        // Generic (systemd-nspawn, lxc, etc.)
        return true;
    }
    if std::env::var("KUBERNETES_SERVICE_HOST").is_ok() {
        // Kubernetes
        return true;
    }

    // Check parent process name for container init systems
    let parent_name = get_parent_process_name();
    match parent_name.as_str() {
        "tini" | "dumb-init" | "s6-svscan" | "runsv" | "runsvdir" | "catatonit" => {
            return true;
        }
        n if n == "{project_name}" => {
            // Parent is our own binary - likely container entrypoint
            return true;
        }
        _ => {}
    }

    // Check cgroup for container indicators
    if let Ok(content) = fs::read_to_string("/proc/1/cgroup") {
        if content.contains("docker")
            || content.contains("kubepods")
            || content.contains("lxc")
        {
            return true;
        }
    }

    false
}

#[derive(Debug, PartialEq)]
pub enum ServiceManager {
    Systemd,
    Launchd,
    Runit,
    S6,
    SysV,
    Rcd,
    Container,
    Manual,
}

// detect_service_manager returns the active service manager
pub fn detect_service_manager() -> ServiceManager {
    if is_container() {
        return ServiceManager::Container;
    }

    let ppid = unsafe { libc::getppid() } as u32;

    // systemd: parent is systemd or PPID=1 with systemd running
    if ppid == 1 && Path::new("/run/systemd/system").exists() {
        return ServiceManager::Systemd;
    }
    // Also check INVOCATION_ID (set by systemd)
    if std::env::var("INVOCATION_ID").is_ok() {
        return ServiceManager::Systemd;
    }

    // launchd: macOS with PPID=1
    #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
    if ppid == 1 {
        return ServiceManager::Launchd;
    }

    // runit: check for SVDIR
    if std::env::var("SVDIR").is_ok() {
        return ServiceManager::Runit;
    }

    // s6: check for S6_* vars
    if std::env::var("S6_LOGGING").is_ok() {
        return ServiceManager::S6;
    }

    // SysV init: /etc/init.d script, no systemd
    if ppid == 1
        && Path::new("/etc/init.d").exists()
        && !Path::new("/run/systemd/system").exists()
    {
        return ServiceManager::SysV;
    }

    // rc.d (BSD): check for rc.subr
    if Path::new("/etc/rc.subr").exists() {
        return ServiceManager::Rcd;
    }

    ServiceManager::Manual
}

// get_parent_process_name returns the name of the parent process
pub fn get_parent_process_name() -> String {
    let ppid = unsafe { libc::getppid() };

    // Linux: read /proc/{ppid}/comm
    if let Ok(data) = fs::read_to_string(format!("/proc/{}/comm", ppid)) {
        return data.trim().to_string();
    }

    // macOS/BSD: use ps command
    if let Ok(output) = std::process::Command::new("ps")
        .args(["-p", &ppid.to_string(), "-o", "comm="])
        .output()
    {
        return String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).trim().to_string();
    }

    String::new()
}

// should_daemonize determines if we should daemonize based on context
pub fn should_daemonize(
    is_service_start: bool,
    daemon_flag: bool,
    config_daemonize: bool,
) -> bool {
    if is_service_start {
        return match detect_service_manager() {
            ServiceManager::Systemd
            | ServiceManager::Launchd
            | ServiceManager::Runit
            | ServiceManager::S6
            | ServiceManager::Container => false,
            ServiceManager::SysV | ServiceManager::Rcd => true,
            ServiceManager::Manual => false,
        };
    }

    // Manual start - respect flag and config
    if daemon_flag {
        return true;
    }
    config_daemonize
}
```

**When to use `--daemon` (manual start only):**
- Starting from terminal without service manager
- Running from cron/at
- Quick testing without terminal lock

**When NOT to use `--daemon`:**
- Any `--service start` scenario (auto-detected)
- Docker/containers (use entrypoint)
- systemd/launchd/runit/s6 (they manage it)

**Daemonization Process (Unix):**

```rust
use std::env;
use std::process;
use anyhow::Result;

// daemonize forks the process and detaches from terminal (Unix)
#[cfg(unix)]
pub fn daemonize(lang: &str) -> Result<()> {
    // Already daemonized? Check if parent is init (PID 1)
    if unsafe { libc::getppid() } == 1 {
        return Ok(());
    }

    // We are the child - continue execution
    if env::var("_DAEMON_CHILD").is_ok() {
        return Ok(());
    }

    // Get our own executable path
    let exec_path = std::fs::read_link("/proc/self/exe")
