**CLI Setup Wizard Flow:**

```
User double-clicks {project_name}-cli:

1. Check for config file (cli.yml)
   в”ңв”Җ Config exists with valid server URL?
   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җ YES вҶ’ Proceed to TUI/GUI app
   в””в”Җ NO config or no server URL?
       в””в”Җ Launch setup wizard

2. Setup wizard:
   a. Prompt for server URL
   b. Test connection to server
      в”ңв”Җ Success вҶ’ Save to config, proceed
      в””в”Җ Fail вҶ’ Show error, retry or exit
   c. Optional: authenticate (if server requires)
   d. Save configuration

3. Launch full TUI/GUI application
```

**Agent First-Run (NO setup wizard):**

```
Agent is configured via server-provided connection string:

{project_name}-agent --connect "https://api.example.com?token=agt_xxx&name=office-pc"

First run without connection string:
- Show error: "No connection configured. Use --connect flag with server-provided URL."
- Agent does NOT have interactive setup wizard
```

**Platform-Specific Double-Click Behavior:**

| Platform | Server | CLI | Agent |
|----------|--------|-----|-------|
| **Windows** | Console opens, banner shown, runs in foreground | Console opens, setup wizard or TUI | Console opens, error (needs --connect) |
| **macOS (Terminal)** | Banner shown, runs in foreground | Setup wizard or TUI | Error (needs --connect) |
| **macOS (Finder)** | вҡ пёҸ Opens Terminal briefly, closes | вҡ пёҸ Opens Terminal briefly, closes | вҡ пёҸ Opens Terminal briefly, closes |
| **Linux (Terminal)** | Banner shown, runs in foreground | Setup wizard or TUI | Error (needs --connect) |
| **Linux (File Manager)** | Depends on DE settings | Depends on DE settings | Depends on DE settings |

**macOS Finder Note:** Raw binaries don't persist when double-clicked from Finder. Users should either:
1. Run from Terminal: `./projectname-cli`
2. Use the `.app` bundle (if GUI build is available)
3. Install via Homebrew and run from Terminal

**Setup Wizard Implementation:**

```rust
// src/client/setup/wizard.rs

use crate::common::config::{load_cli_config, save_cli_config, CliConfig};
use thiserror::Error;

#[derive(Debug, Error)]
pub enum SetupError {
    #[error("setup cancelled by user")]
    Cancelled,
    #[error("config error: {0}")]
    Config(#[from] crate::config::ConfigError),
    #[error("io error: {0}")]
    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),
}

/// Launches interactive setup if no server configured.
pub fn run_setup_wizard() -> Result<(), SetupError> {
    if let Ok(cfg) = load_cli_config() {
        if !cfg.server.primary.is_empty() {
            // Config exists with server вҖ” skip wizard
            return Ok(());
        }
    }

    // Launch TUI setup wizard
    let result = run_wizard_tui()?;
    if result.cancelled {
        return Err(SetupError::Cancelled);
    }

    save_cli_config(&CliConfig {
        server: crate::config::ServerConfig {
            primary: result.server_url,
            ..Default::default()
        },
        ..Default::default()
    })?;

    Ok(())
}

/// Called at CLI startup only. Agent uses --connect flag, not wizard.
pub fn ensure_configured() -> Result<(), SetupError> {
    let cfg = load_cli_config().unwrap_or_default();
    if cfg.server.primary.is_empty() {
        return run_setup_wizard();
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

### GUI Mode Requirements

**When GUI mode is available, use NATIVE platform toolkit. No Electron, no web views.**

| Platform | Toolkit | Notes |
|----------|---------|-------|
| **Linux** | GTK4 or Qt6 | GTK4 preferred for GNOME, Qt6 for KDE |
| **macOS** | Cocoa (AppKit) | Native macOS UI via objc crate |
| **Windows** | Win32/WinUI | Native Windows UI |
| **BSD** | GTK4 or Qt6 | Same as Linux |

```rust
// src/client/gui/mod.rs
// Compiled only with: cargo build --features gui

#[cfg(feature = "gui")]
pub mod gui {
    use crate::client::display::DetectDisplayEnv;

    pub fn is_gui_available() -> bool {
        let env = DetectDisplayEnv::detect();
        env.has_display && !env.is_ssh && !env.is_mosh
    }

    pub fn launch_gui(config: &crate::config::Config) -> Result<(), GuiError> {
        #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
        return launch_gtk_gui(config);

        #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
        return launch_cocoa_gui(config);

        #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
        return launch_win32_gui(config);

        #[cfg(not(any(
            target_os = "linux",
            target_os = "macos",
            target_os = "windows"
        )))]
        {
            // BSD: falls through to GTK
            launch_gtk_gui(config)
        }
    }
}
```

### Platform-Specific GUI Launchers

```rust
// src/client/gui/gui_linux.rs
// Compiled only on Linux with gui feature

#[cfg(all(target_os = "linux", feature = "gui"))]
pub mod linux_gui {
    use gtk4::prelude::*;
    use gtk4::{Application, ApplicationWindow, Box as GtkBox, HeaderBar, Orientation};

    pub fn launch_gtk_gui(config: &crate::config::Config) -> Result<(), super::GuiError> {
        let app = Application::builder()
            .application_id("{plist_name}.cli")
            .build();

        app.connect_activate(move |app| {
            let win = ApplicationWindow::builder()
                .application(app)
                .title("{PROJECT_NAME} CLI")
                .default_width(800)
                .default_height(600)
                .build();

            build_main_window(&win, config);

            win.present();
        });

        app.run();
        Ok(())
    }

    fn build_main_window(win: &ApplicationWindow, _config: &crate::config::Config) {
        let content = GtkBox::new(Orientation::Vertical, 10);
        content.set_margin_top(10);
        content.set_margin_bottom(10);
        content.set_margin_start(10);
        content.set_margin_end(10);

        let header = HeaderBar::new();
        header.set_show_title_buttons(true);
        win.set_titlebar(Some(&header));

        win.set_child(Some(&content));
    }
}
```

```rust
// src/client/gui/gui_macos.rs
// Compiled only on macOS with gui feature

#[cfg(all(target_os = "macos", feature = "gui"))]
pub mod macos_gui {
    use objc2::runtime::NSObject;
    use objc2_app_kit::{NSApplication, NSApplicationActivationPolicy, NSWindow};
    use objc2_foundation::NSString;

    pub fn launch_cocoa_gui(_config: &crate::config::Config) -> Result<(), super::GuiError> {
        unsafe {
            let app = NSApplication::sharedApplication();
            app.setActivationPolicy(
                NSApplicationActivationPolicy::NSApplicationActivationPolicyRegular,
            );

            let title = NSString::from_str("{PROJECT_NAME} CLI");

            // Window setup delegated to AppDelegate implementation
            // See objc2 AppDelegate pattern for full implementation
            let _ = title;

            app.run();
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}

// Objective-C AppDelegate (in build.rs or separate .m file via cc crate):
// @interface AppDelegate : NSObject <NSApplicationDelegate>
// @property (strong) NSWindow *window;
// @end
//
// @implementation AppDelegate
// - (void)applicationDidFinishLaunching:(NSNotification *)notification {
//     NSRect frame = NSMakeRect(0, 0, 800, 600);
//     self.window = [[NSWindow alloc] initWithContentRect:frame
//         styleMask:(NSWindowStyleMaskTitled | NSWindowStyleMaskClosable |
//                    NSWindowStyleMaskResizable | NSWindowStyleMaskMiniaturizable)
//         backing:NSBackingStoreBuffered defer:NO];
//     [self.window setTitle:@"{PROJECT_NAME} CLI"];
//     [self.window center];
//     [self.window makeKeyAndOrderFront:nil];
// }
// @end
```

```rust
// src/client/gui/gui_windows.rs
// Compiled only on Windows with gui feature

#[cfg(all(target_os = "windows", feature = "gui"))]
pub mod windows_gui {
    use windows::Win32::Foundation::{HWND, LPARAM, LRESULT, WPARAM};
    use windows::Win32::System::LibraryLoader::GetModuleHandleW;
    use windows::Win32::UI::WindowsAndMessaging::{
        CreateWindowExW, DefWindowProcW, DispatchMessageW, GetMessageW, MSG,
        PostQuitMessage, RegisterClassExW, ShowWindow, TranslateMessage, UpdateWindow,
        WNDCLASSEXW, WM_DESTROY, WS_OVERLAPPEDWINDOW, WS_VISIBLE, SW_SHOW,
    };
    use windows::core::PCWSTR;

    pub fn launch_win32_gui(_config: &crate::config::Config) -> Result<(), super::GuiError> {
        unsafe {
            let class_name: Vec<u16> = "{project_name}_cli_window\0".encode_utf16().collect();
            let window_name: Vec<u16> = "{PROJECT_NAME} CLI\0".encode_utf16().collect();

            let hinstance = GetModuleHandleW(None)?;

            let wc = WNDCLASSEXW {
                cbSize: std::mem::size_of::<WNDCLASSEXW>() as u32,
                lpfnWndProc: Some(wnd_proc),
                hInstance: hinstance.into(),
                lpszClassName: PCWSTR(class_name.as_ptr()),
                ..Default::default()
            };
            RegisterClassExW(&wc);

            let hwnd = CreateWindowExW(
                Default::default(),
                PCWSTR(class_name.as_ptr()),
                PCWSTR(window_name.as_ptr()),
                WS_OVERLAPPEDWINDOW | WS_VISIBLE,
                100, 100, 800, 600,
                None,
                None,
                hinstance,
                None,
            )?;

            ShowWindow(hwnd, SW_SHOW);
            UpdateWindow(hwnd);

            let mut msg = MSG::default();
            while GetMessageW(&mut msg, None, 0, 0).as_bool() {
                TranslateMessage(&msg);
                DispatchMessageW(&msg);
            }
        }

        Ok(())
    }

    unsafe extern "system" fn wnd_proc(
        hwnd: HWND,
        msg: u32,
        wparam: WPARAM,
        lparam: LPARAM,
    ) -> LRESULT {
        match msg {
            WM_DESTROY => {
                PostQuitMessage(0);
                LRESULT(0)
            }
            _ => DefWindowProcW(hwnd, msg, wparam, lparam),
        }
    }
}
```

```rust
// src/client/gui/gui_bsd.rs
// BSD uses the same GTK implementation as Linux

#[cfg(all(
    any(target_os = "freebsd", target_os = "openbsd", target_os = "netbsd"),
    feature = "gui"
))]
pub mod bsd_gui {
    pub use super::linux_gui::launch_gtk_gui;
}
```

### SSH/Mosh Detection

**Remote sessions ALWAYS use TUI or CLI. Never attempt GUI over SSH/mosh.**

```rust
pub fn is_remote_session() -> bool {
    let ssh_client = std::env::var("SSH_CLIENT").is_ok();
    let ssh_tty = std::env::var("SSH_TTY").is_ok();
    let ssh_connection = std::env::var("SSH_CONNECTION").is_ok();
    let mosh = std::env::var("MOSH").is_ok();

    ssh_client || ssh_tty || ssh_connection || mosh
}
```

## CLI/TUI/GUI Theming

**See PART 16 "Themes (NON-NEGOTIABLE - PROJECT-WIDE)" for the unified color palette.**

**CLI/TUI/GUI use the same `ThemePalette` from `src/common/theme/colors.rs`.**

### CLI Theme Configuration

```yaml
# cli.yml - theme section (same format as server.yml)
theme:
  mode: auto          # dark (default), light, auto
```

### TUI Styles from Palette

```rust
// src/client/tui/styles.rs

use ratatui::style::{Color, Modifier, Style};
use crate::common::theme::ThemePalette;

/// TUI styles derived from ThemePalette.
pub struct TuiStyles {
    pub base: Style,
    pub title: Style,
    pub selected: Style,
    pub error: Style,
    pub success: Style,
    pub warning: Style,
    pub muted: Style,
    pub border: Style,
}

impl TuiStyles {
    pub fn from_palette(p: &ThemePalette) -> Self {
        Self {
            base: Style::default()
                .fg(hex_to_color(&p.foreground))
                .bg(hex_to_color(&p.background)),
            title: Style::default()
                .fg(hex_to_color(&p.primary))
                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
            selected: Style::default()
                .fg(hex_to_color(&p.background))
                .bg(hex_to_color(&p.primary)),
            error: Style::default().fg(hex_to_color(&p.error)),
            success: Style::default().fg(hex_to_color(&p.success)),
            warning: Style::default().fg(hex_to_color(&p.warning)),
            muted: Style::default().fg(hex_to_color(&p.muted)),
            border: Style::default().fg(hex_to_color(&p.border)),
        }
    }
}

fn hex_to_color(hex: &str) -> Color {
    let hex = hex.trim_start_matches('#');
    let r = u8::from_str_radix(&hex[0..2], 16).unwrap_or(255);
    let g = u8::from_str_radix(&hex[2..4], 16).unwrap_or(255);
    let b = u8::from_str_radix(&hex[4..6], 16).unwrap_or(255);
    Color::Rgb(r, g, b)
}
```

### CLI Colored Output

```rust
// src/client/cli/output.rs

use crate::common::theme::ThemePalette;

pub struct Output {
    colors: bool,
    palette: ThemePalette,
}

impl Output {
    pub fn print_success(&self, msg: &str) {
        if self.colors {
            let (r, g, b) = hex_to_rgb(&self.palette.success);
            println!("\x1b[38;2;{r};{g};{b}m{msg}\x1b[0m");
        } else {
            println!("{msg}");
        }
    }

    pub fn print_error(&self, msg: &str) {
        if self.colors {
            let (r, g, b) = hex_to_rgb(&self.palette.error);
            println!("\x1b[38;2;{r};{g};{b}m{msg}\x1b[0m");
        } else {
            println!("{msg}");
        }
    }
}

fn hex_to_rgb(hex: &str) -> (u8, u8, u8) {
    let hex = hex.trim_start_matches('#');
    let r = u8::from_str_radix(&hex[0..2], 16).unwrap_or(255);
    let g = u8::from_str_radix(&hex[2..4], 16).unwrap_or(255);
    let b = u8::from_str_radix(&hex[4..6], 16).unwrap_or(255);
    (r, g, b)
}
```

## Responsive Layout

**ALL interfaces MUST look professional from mobile (40x10) to 16K displays (400+ columns).**

### Screen Size Categories

| Category | Columns | Rows | Examples | Layout |
|----------|---------|------|----------|--------|
| **Micro** | <40 | <10 | Phone portrait SSH | Vertical stack, no chrome |
| **Minimal** | 40-59 | 10-15 | Phone landscape SSH | Single column, minimal borders |
| **Compact** | 60-79 | 16-23 | Tablet SSH, small terminal | Compact layout, abbreviated |
| **Standard** | 80-119 | 24-39 | Standard terminal | Normal layout |
| **Wide** | 120-199 | 40-59 | Wide terminal | Sidebars, multi-column |
| **Ultrawide** | 200-399 | 60+ | Ultrawide monitor | Three-column, dashboards |
| **Massive** | 400+ | 80+ | 4K/8K/16K displays | Multi-pane, tile layout |

### GUI Responsive Layout

```rust
// GUI window scaling for high-DPI and large displays
pub struct Layout {
    pub mode: &'static str,
    pub columns: u32,
    pub sidebar: bool,
    pub sidebar_w: u32,
    pub font_scale: f64,
}

pub fn calculate_gui_layout(width: u32, height: u32, dpi: f64) -> Layout {
    // Scale based on DPI вҖ” 96 DPI is baseline
    let scale = dpi / 96.0;

    match width {
        w if w >= 3840 => Layout {
            mode: "dashboard",
            columns: 4,
            sidebar: true,
            sidebar_w: (300.0 * scale) as u32,
            font_scale: scale,
        },
        w if w >= 2560 => Layout {
            mode: "wide",
            columns: 3,
            sidebar: true,
            sidebar_w: (280.0 * scale) as u32,
            font_scale: scale,
        },
        w if w >= 1920 => Layout {
            mode: "standard",
            columns: 2,
            sidebar: true,
            sidebar_w: (250.0 * scale) as u32,
            font_scale: scale,
        },
        w if w >= 1280 => Layout {
            mode: "compact",
            columns: 1,
            sidebar: true,
            sidebar_w: (200.0 * scale) as u32,
            font_scale: scale,
        },
        _ => Layout {
            mode: "mobile",
            columns: 1,
            sidebar: false,
            sidebar_w: 0,
            font_scale: scale,
        },
    }
}
```

### TUI Responsive Layout

**TUI uses `terminal::SizeMode` from `src/common/terminal` (see PART 7) for size detection.**

```rust
// src/client/tui/layout.rs

use crate::common::terminal::SizeMode;

/// TUI-specific layout settings based on SizeMode.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct LayoutConfig {
    pub show_borders: bool,
    pub show_header: bool,
    pub show_footer: bool,
    pub show_sidebar: bool,
    pub sidebar_width: u16,
    pub max_columns: u16,
    pub truncate_at: u16,
    pub use_abbrev: bool,
    pub vertical_scroll: bool,
    pub multi_pane: bool,
    pub tile_layout: bool,
}

/// Returns layout config for a given SizeMode.
pub fn get_layout_config(mode: SizeMode) -> LayoutConfig {
    match mode {
        SizeMode::Micro => LayoutConfig {
            show_borders: false,
            show_header: false,
            show_footer: false,
            show_sidebar: false,
            sidebar_width: 0,
            max_columns: 2,
            truncate_at: 30,
            use_abbrev: true,
            vertical_scroll: true,
            multi_pane: false,
            tile_layout: false,
        },
        SizeMode::Minimal => LayoutConfig {
            show_borders: false,
            show_header: true,
            show_footer: true,
            show_sidebar: false,
            sidebar_width: 0,
            max_columns: 3,
            truncate_at: 40,
            use_abbrev: true,
            vertical_scroll: true,
            multi_pane: false,
            tile_layout: false,
        },
        SizeMode::Compact => LayoutConfig {
            show_borders: true,
            show_header: true,
            show_footer: true,
            show_sidebar: false,
            sidebar_width: 0,
            max_columns: 4,
            truncate_at: 60,
            use_abbrev: false,
            vertical_scroll: true,
            multi_pane: false,
            tile_layout: false,
        },
        SizeMode::Standard => LayoutConfig {
            show_borders: true,
            show_header: true,
            show_footer: true,
            show_sidebar: false,
            sidebar_width: 0,
            max_columns: 6,
            truncate_at: 80,
            use_abbrev: false,
            vertical_scroll: true,
            multi_pane: false,
            tile_layout: false,
        },
        SizeMode::Wide => LayoutConfig {
            show_borders: true,
            show_header: true,
            show_footer: true,
            show_sidebar: true,
            sidebar_width: 30,
            max_columns: 8,
            truncate_at: 120,
            use_abbrev: false,
            vertical_scroll: true,
            multi_pane: false,
            tile_layout: false,
        },
        SizeMode::Ultrawide => LayoutConfig {
            show_borders: true,
            show_header: true,
            show_footer: true,
            show_sidebar: true,
            sidebar_width: 40,
            max_columns: 12,
            truncate_at: 200,
            use_abbrev: false,
            // Full content visible
            vertical_scroll: false,
            multi_pane: true,
            tile_layout: false,
        },
        SizeMode::Massive => LayoutConfig {
            show_borders: true,
            show_header: true,
            show_footer: true,
            show_sidebar: true,
            sidebar_width: 50,
            max_columns: 20,
            // No truncation
            truncate_at: 0,
            use_abbrev: false,
            vertical_scroll: false,
            multi_pane: true,
            tile_layout: true,
        },
    }
}
```

### Window Resize Handling

**ALL modes MUST handle window resize smoothly.**

```rust
// TUI resize handling with ratatui + crossterm

use crossterm::event::{Event, KeyCode};
use crate::common::terminal::{get_terminal_size, SizeMode};

pub struct Model {
    pub width: u16,
    pub height: u16,
    pub size_mode: SizeMode,
    pub layout: LayoutConfig,
    pub debug: bool,
}

impl Model {
    pub fn handle_event(&mut self, event: Event) {
        if let Event::Resize(width, height) = event {
            self.width = width;
            self.height = height;

            let size = get_terminal_size();
            self.size_mode = size.mode;
            self.layout = get_layout_config(size.mode);

            self.recalculate_viewport();
            self.recalculate_tables();
            self.recalculate_sidebar();

            if self.debug {
                tracing::debug!(
                    width,
                    height,
                    mode = ?self.size_mode,
                    "window resize"
                );
            }
        }
    }

    fn recalculate_viewport(&mut self) { /* ... */ }
    fn recalculate_tables(&mut self) { /* ... */ }
    fn recalculate_sidebar(&mut self) { /* ... */ }
}

// Server/Agent: Handle window resize for console output

// Unix: react to SIGWINCH
#[cfg(unix)]
pub async fn watch_window_size<F>(mut ctx: tokio::sync::watch::Receiver<()>, callback: F)
where
    F: Fn(u16, u16) + Send + 'static,
{
    use tokio::signal::unix::{signal, SignalKind};

    let mut sigwinch = signal(SignalKind::window_change())
        .expect("failed to install SIGWINCH handler");

    loop {
        tokio::select! {
            _ = ctx.changed() => break,
            _ = sigwinch.recv() => {
                if let Ok((w, h)) = crossterm::terminal::size() {
                    callback(w, h);
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

// Windows: poll for size changes (no SIGWINCH)
#[cfg(windows)]
pub async fn watch_window_size<F>(mut ctx: tokio::sync::watch::Receiver<()>, callback: F)
where
    F: Fn(u16, u16) + Send + 'static,
{
    use tokio::time::{interval, Duration};

    let mut ticker = interval(Duration::from_millis(500));
    let mut last_w = 0u16;
    let mut last_h = 0u16;

    loop {
        tokio::select! {
            _ = ctx.changed() => break,
            _ = ticker.tick() => {
                if let Ok((w, h)) = crossterm::terminal::size() {
                    if w != last_w || h != last_h {
                        last_w = w;
                        last_h = h;
                        callback(w, h);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## Professional UI/UX Standards

**Every interface MUST look professional. No amateur hour.**

### Visual Hierarchy

| Element | Priority | Styling |
|---------|----------|---------|
| **Primary Actions** | High | Primary color, prominent placement |
| **Data/Content** | High | Clear contrast, readable fonts |
| **Navigation** | Medium | Consistent position, clear labels |
| **Secondary Actions** | Medium | Muted styling, discoverable |
| **Status/Meta** | Low | Muted color, smaller text |
| **Decorative** | Lowest | Subtle, never distracting |

### Spacing and Alignment

```rust
// Consistent spacing units (TUI)
pub const SPACE_XS: u16 = 1;
pub const SPACE_S: u16 = 2;
pub const SPACE_M: u16 = 4;
pub const SPACE_L: u16 = 6;
pub const SPACE_XL: u16 = 8;

/// Returns the appropriate spacing constant for a given terminal size mode.
pub fn get_spacing_for_mode(mode: crate::common::terminal::SizeMode) -> u16 {
    use crate::common::terminal::SizeMode;
    match mode {
        SizeMode::Micro | SizeMode::Minimal => SPACE_XS,
        SizeMode::Compact => SPACE_S,
        SizeMode::Standard => SPACE_M,
        SizeMode::Wide => SPACE_L,
        _ => SPACE_XL,
    }
}
```

### Consistent Icons and Symbols

```rust
// src/client/tui/symbols.rs

/// Unicode symbols that work across terminals.
pub struct TuiSymbols {
    pub success: &'static str,
    pub error: &'static str,
    pub warning: &'static str,
    pub info: &'static str,
    pub arrow: &'static str,
    pub check: &'static str,
    pub cross: &'static str,
    pub bullet: &'static str,
    pub spinner: &'static [&'static str],
}

pub const UNICODE_SYMBOLS: TuiSymbols = TuiSymbols {
    success: "вң“",
    error: "вң—",
    warning: "вҡ ",
    info: "в„№",
    arrow: "вҶ’",
    check: "вҳ‘",
    cross: "вҳ’",
    bullet: "вҖў",
    spinner: &["в Ӣ", "в ҷ", "в №", "в ё", "в ј", "в ҙ", "в Ұ", "в §", "в Ү", "в Ҹ"],
};

/// Fallback for non-Unicode terminals.
pub const ASCII_SYMBOLS: TuiSymbols = TuiSymbols {
    success: "[OK]",
    error: "[ERR]",
    warning: "[WARN]",
    info: "[INFO]",
    arrow: "->",
    check: "[x]",
    cross: "[ ]",
    bullet: "*",
    spinner: &["|", "/", "-", "\\"],
};

pub fn get_tui_symbols(env: &crate::client::display::DisplayEnv) -> &'static TuiSymbols {
    if env.supports_unicode() {
        &UNICODE_SYMBOLS
    } else {
        &ASCII_SYMBOLS
    }
}
```

When command arguments are provided:

# Standard CLI output (your project)
{project_name}-cli create --file notes.txt --expire 24h
{project_name}-cli get abc123 --output json
{project_name}-cli list --limit 10
{project_name}-cli search --query "keyword"
```

### TUI Mode (Automatic)

When launched with no arguments in an interactive terminal:

```bash
# Launch TUI (no arguments needed)

# TUI provides:
# - Interactive menus
# - Search with live results
# - Keyboard shortcuts
# - Visual data display
# - Professional, polished interface
```

**TUI Library:** Use `ratatui` + `crossterm` for TUI implementation.

## Configuration

### Directory Structure

**Client runtime state is always user-scope. The CLI binary may be installed system-wide, but config/data/cache/logs MUST stay in the invoking user's XDG/profile directories.**

The CLI binary:
- Runs in the invoking user's context without privilege escalation
- Uses user/home/profile directories exclusively, even if the invoking user happens to be root/Administrator
- May be installed in a shared system PATH location (for example `/usr/local/bin`) so one binary is available to all users
- Creates all directories and files itself (no external scripts/installers)
- Sets permissions and ownership before creating any files
- Works identically across all platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows)

The client uses the same user directory structure as the server in user mode. This allows the client to share configuration with a locally running server when appropriate.

#### Linux / macOS

| Directory | Path | Purpose |
|-----------|------|---------|
| Config | `~/.config/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` | Configuration files |
| Config File | `~/.config/{project_org}/{internal_name}/cli.yml` | CLI configuration |
| Data | `~/.local/share/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` | Persistent data |
| Cache | `~/.cache/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` | Temporary/cached data |
| Logs | `~/.local/log/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` | Log files |
| Log File | `~/.local/log/{project_org}/{internal_name}/cli.log` | CLI log output |

#### Windows

| Directory | Path | Purpose |
|-----------|------|---------|
| Config | `%APPDATA%\{project_org}\{internal_name}\` | Configuration files |
| Config File | `%APPDATA%\{project_org}\{internal_name}\cli.yml` | CLI configuration |
| Data | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\{project_org}\{internal_name}\data\` | Persistent data |
| Cache | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\{project_org}\{internal_name}\cache\` | Temporary/cached data |
| Logs | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\{project_org}\{internal_name}\log\` | Log files |
| Log File | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\{project_org}\{internal_name}\log\cli.log` | CLI log output |

#### Directory Usage

| Directory | Contents | Backup? |
|-----------|----------|---------|
| Config | `cli.yml`, connection profiles, preferences | Yes |
| Data | Downloaded data, local databases, saved items | Yes |
| Cache | API response cache, temp files, thumbnails | No (recreatable) |
| Logs | `cli.log`, debug logs | Optional |

**NEVER use OS system directories for CLI runtime state:**
- `/etc/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` (Linux system config)
- `/var/lib/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` (Linux system data)
- `/var/log/{project_org}/{internal_name}/` (Linux system logs)
- `C:\ProgramData\` (Windows system data)
- Any other system-owned directory for CLI config/data/cache/logs

#### CLI Startup Sequence

**The CLI binary handles ALL initialization. No external scripts, no installers, no manual setup.**

On every startup, the CLI MUST:

1. **Ensure directories exist** (create if missing):
   ```
   в”ңв”Җ {config_dir}/           (~/.config/{project_org}/{internal_name}/)
   в”ңв”Җ {data_dir}/             (~/.local/share/{project_org}/{internal_name}/)
   в”ңв”Җ {cache_dir}/            (~/.cache/{project_org}/{internal_name}/)
   в””в”Җ {log_dir}/              (~/.local/log/{project_org}/{internal_name}/)
   ```

2. **Set correct permissions** (user-only access):
   - Directories: `0700` (rwx------)
   - Config files: `0600` (rw-------)
   - Log files: `0600` (rw-------)

3. **Verify ownership** (current user):
   - All directories and files owned by the invoking user account for that run
   - Never require system ownership or privileged/system directories just because the invoking user is root/admin

```rust
// src/client/init.rs

use std::fs;
use std::path::Path;
use thiserror::Error;

use crate::client::paths;

#[derive(Debug, Error)]
pub enum InitError {
    #[error("failed to create directory {path}: {source}")]
    CreateDir {
        path: String,
        #[source]
        source: std::io::Error,
    },
    #[error("failed to set permissions on {path}: {source}")]
    SetPerms {
        path: String,
        #[source]
        source: std::io::Error,
    },
}

/// Creates all CLI directories with correct permissions.
/// Called on every startup before any file operations.
pub fn ensure_dirs() -> Result<(), InitError> {
    let dirs = [
        paths::config_dir(),
        paths::data_dir(),
        paths::cache_dir(),
        paths::log_dir(),
    ];

    for dir in &dirs {
        fs::create_dir_all(dir).map_err(|e| InitError::CreateDir {
            path: dir.display().to_string(),
            source: e,
        })?;
        set_dir_permissions(dir).map_err(|e| InitError::SetPerms {
            path: dir.display().to_string(),
            source: e,
        })?;
    }

    Ok(())
}

#[cfg(unix)]
fn set_dir_permissions(dir: &Path) -> std::io::Result<()> {
    use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;
    // Unix: ensure 0700 (user-only access)
    fs::set_permissions(dir, fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o700))
}

#[cfg(unix)]
pub fn set_file_permissions(path: &Path) -> std::io::Result<()> {
    use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;
    // Unix: ensure 0600 (user-only read/write)
    fs::set_permissions(path, fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o600))
}

#[cfg(windows)]
fn set_dir_permissions(_dir: &Path) -> std::io::Result<()> {
    // Windows: ACLs are inherited from parent by default in user directories.
    // %APPDATA% and %LOCALAPPDATA% already have user-only access.
    // No explicit ACL modification needed for user directories.
    Ok(())
}

#[cfg(windows)]
pub fn set_file_permissions(_path: &Path) -> std::io::Result<()> {
    // Windows: files inherit ACLs from directory.
    // For sensitive files in user directories this is sufficient.
    Ok(())
}

/// Creates parent directories and sets permissions before file creation.
/// MUST be called before any file creation.
pub fn ensure_file(path: &Path) -> Result<(), InitError> {
    let dir = path.parent().unwrap_or(Path::new("."));
    fs::create_dir_all(dir).map_err(|e| InitError::CreateDir {
        path: dir.display().to_string(),
        source: e,
    })?;
    Ok(())
}
```

**CLI initialization order:**
1. Parse CLI flags (clap)
2. Call `ensure_dirs()` вҖ” create all directories
3. Load/create config file (`cli.yml`)
4. Initialize logging (`cli.log`)
5. Execute command

#### Path Resolution (Rust)

```rust
// src/client/paths/mod.rs

use std::path::PathBuf;

const PROJECT_ORG: &str = "{project_org}";
const PROJECT_NAME: &str = "{project_name}";

/// Returns the CLI config directory.
pub fn config_dir() -> PathBuf {
    #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
    {
        let appdata = std::env::var("APPDATA").unwrap_or_default();
        PathBuf::from(appdata).join(PROJECT_ORG).join(PROJECT_NAME)
    }
    #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
    {
        home_dir().join(".config").join(PROJECT_ORG).join(PROJECT_NAME)
    }
}

/// Returns the CLI data directory.
pub fn data_dir() -> PathBuf {
    #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
    {
        let localappdata = std::env::var("LOCALAPPDATA").unwrap_or_default();
        PathBuf::from(localappdata)
            .join(PROJECT_ORG)
            .join(PROJECT_NAME)
            .join("data")
    }
    #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
    {
        home_dir()
            .join(".local")
            .join("share")
            .join(PROJECT_ORG)
            .join(PROJECT_NAME)
    }
}

/// Returns the CLI cache directory.
pub fn cache_dir() -> PathBuf {
    #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
    {
        let localappdata = std::env::var("LOCALAPPDATA").unwrap_or_default();
        PathBuf::from(localappdata)
            .join(PROJECT_ORG)
            .join(PROJECT_NAME)
            .join("cache")
    }
    #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
    {
        home_dir().join(".cache").join(PROJECT_ORG).join(PROJECT_NAME)
    }
}

/// Returns the CLI log directory.
pub fn log_dir() -> PathBuf {
    #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
    {
        let localappdata = std::env::var("LOCALAPPDATA").unwrap_or_default();
        PathBuf::from(localappdata)
            .join(PROJECT_ORG)
            .join(PROJECT_NAME)
            .join("log")
    }
    #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
    {
        home_dir()
            .join(".local")
            .join("log")
            .join(PROJECT_ORG)
            .join(PROJECT_NAME)
    }
}

/// Returns the CLI config file path.
pub fn config_file() -> PathBuf {
    config_dir().join("cli.yml")
}

/// Returns the CLI log file path.
pub fn log_file() -> PathBuf {
    log_dir().join("cli.log")
}

fn home_dir() -> PathBuf {
    dirs::home_dir().unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("/tmp"))
}
```

### --config Flag (Config File Selection)

**The `--config` flag loads a specific config file instead of the default `cli.yml`.**

| Input | Resolution |
|-------|------------|
| `--config test` | `{config_dir}/test.yml` |
| `--config dev.yml` | `{config_dir}/dev.yml` |
| `--config test.yaml` | `{config_dir}/test.yaml` |
| `--config ~/testing/app.yml` | Absolute path: `~/testing/app.yml` |
| `--config /etc/myapp/prod.yml` | Absolute path: `/etc/myapp/prod.yml` |
| (not specified) | Default: `{config_dir}/cli.yml` |

**Resolution rules:**
1. If path is absolute (starts with `/` or `~`) вҶ’ expand, then check extension
2. If path is relative вҶ’ resolve relative to `{config_dir}`
3. If extension is `.yml` or `.yaml` вҶ’ use as-is
4. If no extension вҶ’ auto-detect: check `.yml` first, then `.yaml`, default to `.yml`
5. File must exist or error (except for new configs which default to `.yml`)

```rust
use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};

/// Resolves the --config flag value to an absolute path.
pub fn resolve_config_path(config_flag: Option<&str>) -> Result<PathBuf, std::io::Error> {
    let Some(flag) = config_flag else {
        return Ok(crate::client::paths::config_file());
    };

    // Expand ~ to home directory
    let expanded = if let Some(rest) = flag.strip_prefix("~/") {
        dirs::home_dir()
            .unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("/tmp"))
            .join(rest)
    } else {
        PathBuf::from(flag)
    };

    // Absolute path вҖ” check extension detection
    if expanded.is_absolute() {
        return Ok(resolve_yaml_extension(&expanded));
    }

    // Relative path вҖ” resolve to config_dir
    let path = crate::client::paths::config_dir().join(&expanded);
    Ok(resolve_yaml_extension(&path))
}

/// Auto-detects .yml or .yaml extension.
/// If no extension provided, checks for existing .yml then .yaml.
/// Defaults to .yml if neither exists (for new configs).
fn resolve_yaml_extension(path: &Path) -> PathBuf {
    let ext = path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()).unwrap_or("");

    if ext == "yml" || ext == "yaml" {
        return path.to_path_buf();
    }

    if ext.is_empty() {
        let yml = path.with_extension("yml");
        if yml.exists() {
            return yml;
        }
        let yaml = path.with_extension("yaml");
        if yaml.exists() {
            return yaml;
        }
        // Default to .yml for new configs
        return path.with_extension("yml");
    }

    // Non-yaml extension вҖ” use as-is
    path.to_path_buf()
}
```

**Example usage:**
```bash
# Use different configs for different environments
{project_name}-cli --config dev list              # Uses ~/.config/.../dev.yml
{project_name}-cli --config staging list          # Uses ~/.config/.../staging.yml
{project_name}-cli --config ~/work/prod.yml list  # Uses absolute path

# Config profiles allow different servers/tokens without flags
# dev.yml:   server: https://dev.example.com, token: dev-token
# staging.yml: server: https://staging.example.com, token: staging-token
```

### cli.yml Configuration

**EVERYTHING must be configurable via cli.yml. Sane defaults match server where applicable.**

```yaml
# ~/.config/{project_org}/{internal_name}/cli.yml
# client configuration - ALL options with defaults

# Server connection
server:
  primary: ""                      # Server URL (empty = use {official_site} or prompt)
  cluster: []                      # Auto-discovered cluster nodes
  api_version: v1                  # API version prefix (default: v1, must match server)
  admin_path: admin                # Admin path (default: admin, must match server)
  timeout: 30s                     # Request timeout (match server default)
  retry: 3                         # Retry attempts on failure
  retry_delay: 1s                  # Delay between retries

# Authentication (required for multi-user, see PART 34/35)
auth:
  token: ""                        # API token (usr_xxx, see PART 11)
  token_file: ""                   # Read token from file instead
  # No default_context - default is ALWAYS token owner (user)
  # Use --user {org} to switch to org context

# Output preferences
output:
  format: table                    # Default: table, json, yaml, plain, csv
  color: auto                      # auto, yes, no (match terminal detection)
  pager: auto                      # auto, always, never (use less/more for long output)
  quiet: false                     # Suppress non-essential output
  verbose: false                   # Extra output (same as --verbose)

# TUI preferences (if TUI supported)
tui:
  enabled: true                    # Allow TUI mode (false = CLI-only)
  theme: dark                      # dark, light, system (match server theme options)
  mouse: true                      # Enable mouse support
  unicode: true                    # Use unicode characters (false = ASCII only)

# Logging
logging:
  level: warn                      # debug, info, warn, error (match server default)
  file: ""                         # Log file path (empty = {log_dir}/cli.log)
  max_size: 10MB                   # Max log file size (match server default)
  max_files: 5                     # Max log files to keep (match server default)

# Cache
cache:
  enabled: true                    # Enable response caching
  ttl: 5m                          # Cache TTL (5 minutes)
  max_size: 100MB                  # Max cache size

# Debug
debug: false                       # Enable debug mode (same as --debug)

# Project-specific defaults (flag defaults)
defaults:
  # These become defaults for project-specific flags
  # Example for pastebin:
  # public: unlisted               # --public default
  # expire: 24h                    # --expire default
  # syntax: auto                   # --syntax default
```

**Config precedence (highest to lowest):**

| Priority | Source | Example |
|----------|--------|---------|
| 1 | CLI flag | `--format json` |
| 2 | Environment variable | `{PROJECT_NAME}_FORMAT=json` |
| 3 | Config file | `output.format: json` |
| 4 | Compiled default | `table` |

**Environment variable mapping:**
```bash
# Pattern: {PROJECT_NAME}_{SECTION}_{KEY} or {PROJECT_NAME}_{KEY}
{PROJECT_NAME}_SERVER_PRIMARY="https://example.com"
{PROJECT_NAME}_SERVER_TIMEOUT=60
{PROJECT_NAME}_TOKEN="usr_abc123..."
{PROJECT_NAME}_OUTPUT_FORMAT="json"
{PROJECT_NAME}_DEBUG=true
```### CLI Cluster Failover

**client MUST support automatic cluster failover. Background node discovery keeps config current.**

| Feature | Behavior |
|---------|----------|
| **Background discovery** | CLI calls `/api/autodiscover` in background |
| **Auto-update** | `server.cluster` updated from `cluster.nodes` in response |
| **Failover** | If primary fails, try cluster nodes transparently |
| **No user action** | Completely automatic, user sees no difference |

**CLI Cluster Behavior:**
```
On every CLI command:
1. Load cli.yml
2. Try server.primary
3. If fails вҶ’ try server.cluster nodes (silent failover)
4. Execute command on first available node
5. Background: GET /api/{api_version}/server/healthz
6. Read cluster.primary and cluster.nodes from response
7. Update server.primary and server.cluster in cli.yml (async, non-blocking)
```

**User Experience:**
- User runs `mycli list` - just works
- If primary is down, CLI silently uses cluster node
- No error unless ALL nodes are unreachable
- Config stays current automatically

### Server Address Resolution

**Priority order (highest to lowest):**

1. `--server` flag (if provided)
2. `server.primary` in `cli.yml` (if set)
3. `server.cluster` nodes (if primary fails)
4. `{official_site}` compiled default (if project has official site)
5. Error with setup instructions

**Behavior:**

| Scenario | Result |
|----------|--------|
| `--server` flag used | Use that server, **save to `server.primary` in cli.yml** |
| `server.primary` in cli.yml | Use configured primary server |
| Primary fails, cluster exists | Silently try cluster nodes |
| Project has `{official_site}` | Use official site as default |
| No server available | Error with setup instructions |

**Config save rule: Validate everything, only save valid, never clear valid.**

```rust
// save_if_empty saves value to config only if current is empty and new is valid
fn save_if_empty(
    current: &str,
    new_value: &str,
    validate: impl Fn(&str) -> bool,
) -> Result<String, String> {
    if new_value.is_empty() {
        return Ok(current.to_string());
    }
    if !validate(new_value) {
        return Err(format!("invalid value: {}", new_value));
    }
    if current.is_empty() {
        return Ok(new_value.to_string()); // Save to config
    }
    Ok(new_value.to_string()) // Use for session, don't save (current preserved)
}
```

| Principle | Rule |
|-----------|------|
| **Validate everything** | Always validate before use or save |
| **Only save valid** | Never save invalid values to config |
| **Never clear valid** | Don't overwrite existing valid config |

**Error when no server configured (projects without official site):**

```bash
$ {project_name}-cli list
Error: no server configured

To configure a server, run:
  {project_name}-cli --server https://your-server.example.com list

This will save the server address for future commands.
Or edit ~/.config/{project_org}/{internal_name}/cli.yml directly.
```

**Projects with official site show default in help:**

```bash
$ {project_name}-cli --help
...
Options:
      --server <URL>    Server address [default: https://{project_name}.example.com]
...
```

**Official site (`{official_site}`) is taken from `site.txt` when that file exists. Otherwise fall back to `IDEA.md ## Project variables` (`official_site`) and other compatibility sources.**

**What official site affects:**
- README.md: Default production site URL in examples
- CLI/Agent: Default `--server` URL (so users don't need to specify)

**What official site does NOT affect:**
- Docker labels (use `{project_org}`, `{project_name}`, `{fqdn}`)
- Documentation structure or content
- Build artifacts or binary metadata
- Any runtime behavior (just a compiled default)

Sources for official site (check in order):
1. `site.txt`: single line URL `https://...`
2. `IDEA.md ## Project variables`: `official_site: https://...`
3. README.md: `Official site is: https://...` or `Site: https://...` (compatibility fallback only)
4. AI asks user (if not found in canonical project files)
5. If user selects "none", project has no default server

**Conflict rule:** if both `site.txt` and `IDEA.md` exist and disagree, `site.txt` wins. Update `IDEA.md` to match or remove the stale `official_site` entry.

**AI should ask if not defined:**
```
Official site not found in site.txt, IDEA.md, or README.md.
What is the official site for this project?

1) https://projectname.example.com
2) https://api.example.com/projectname
3) None (users must configure with --server)
4) Other (specify URL)

Enter choice [1-4]:
```

## Standard Flags

### Universal Flags (ALL Binaries)

| Flag | Short | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| `--help` | `-h` | Show help |
| `--version` | `-v` | Show version |
| `--color {auto\|yes\|no}` | вҖ” | Color output (default: auto, respects `NO_COLOR`) |
| `--lang CODE` | вҖ” | Language for output (default: auto-detect from `LANG` env) |

**Only `-h` and `-v` have short forms. Everything else is long-form only.**

- **No root required** вҖ” `--help` and `--version` must never require escalation; they always run as the calling user regardless of service install state
- **No escalation** вҖ” help at every level (main, subcommand, nested) must never call `sudo`, require root/admin, or check privilege state; exit immediately with the help text.

### Boolean/Truthy-Falsey Handling (CLI & Agent)

**CLI and Agent binaries use the SAME truthy/falsey parsing as the server.**

See PART 5: Boolean Handling for the complete implementation.

| Truthy | Falsey |
|--------|--------|
| `true`, `yes`, `on`, `1`, `enable`, `enabled` | `false`, `no`, `off`, `0`, `disable`, `disabled`, `none` |

**Usage in flags:**
```bash
{project_name}-cli --public                    # Boolean flag (true)
{project_name}-cli --public=yes                # Explicit truthy
{project_name}-cli --public=no                 # Explicit falsey
{project_name}-cli --expire=0                  # Falsey = no expiration
{project_name}-cli --expire=disabled           # Falsey = no expiration
```

**Config file (cli.yml):**
```yaml
server:
  verify_ssl: yes        # Truthy
  auto_update: false     # Falsey
  notifications: enabled # Truthy
```

**ALL boolean inputs MUST use `config::parse_bool()` or `config::is_truthy()` - NEVER `str::parse::<bool>()`.**

### Common Optional Flags (if needed)

| Flag | When to Include |
|------|-----------------|
| `--server` | Multi-server support (skip if single official site) |
| `--token` | Authenticated operations |
| `--config` | Multiple config profiles |
| `--output` | Multiple output formats (json/table/plain) |
| `--debug` | Debug mode |

### Flag Argument Syntax (ALL Binaries)

**All binaries accept BOTH `=` and space for flag values (clap handles both automatically):**
```
--{flag}={value}    # Equals syntax
--{flag} {value}    # Space syntax
```

**Examples:**
```bash
--public=yes    OR  --public yes
--expire=24h    OR  --expire 24h
--limit=10      OR  --limit 10
--output=json   OR  --output json
--color=yes  OR  --color yes
```

### Project-Specific Flags (by service type)

**Search/Query CLI (minimal flags):**
```bash
# Args ARE the search - no flags needed for basic use
{project_name}-cli golang tutorials           # Search
{project_name}-cli --limit 10 golang          # With limit
{project_name}-cli --output json golang       # JSON output
```

**Pastebin/Content CLI:**
```bash
# Smart detection handles input, flags for metadata
{project_name}-cli notes.txt                          # File (detected), uses defaults
{project_name}-cli notes.txt --public yes             # Public paste
{project_name}-cli notes.txt --public no              # Private (requires auth)
{project_name}-cli notes.txt --public unlisted        # Unlisted (default)
{project_name}-cli notes.txt --expire 24h             # Expiration
{project_name}-cli notes.txt --syntax python          # Syntax highlight
{project_name}-cli notes.txt --author "John"          # Author name
```

**API/Data CLI:**
```bash
# Resource-specific flags
{project_name}-cli get abc123                         # Get by ID
{project_name}-cli list --limit 20 --offset 0         # Pagination
{project_name}-cli delete abc123 --force              # Dangerous ops need confirm
```

### Flag Defaults from Config

**Every flag MUST have a corresponding config setting to change its default.**

```yaml
# cli.yml - defaults for flags
defaults:
  lang: auto            # --lang default (auto = detect from env, or "en", "es", etc.)
  public: unlisted      # --public default (yes, no, unlisted)
  expire: 24h           # --expire default
  syntax: auto          # --syntax default
  output: table         # --output default (json, table, plain)
  limit: 20             # --limit default
```

**Precedence (highest to lowest):**
1. Command-line flag (`--public yes`)
2. Environment variable (`{PROJECT_NAME}_PUBLIC=yes`)
3. Config file (`defaults.public: yes`)
4. Hardcoded default

### Flag Guidelines

| Rule | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Minimize flags** | Smart detection > explicit flags |
| **Config defaults** | Every flag has config equivalent |
| **No redundant flags** | If detectable, don't require flag |
| **Value flags** | `--public unlisted` not `--unlisted` |
| **Confirmation** | Destructive ops: `--force` or interactive confirm |

### Shell Completions (Built-in, NON-NEGOTIABLE)

**ALL binaries (server, agent, client) MUST support shell completions. Built into binary - no separate files.**

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--shell completions [SHELL]` | Print completion script to stdout |
| `--shell init [SHELL]` | Alias: prints eval-wrapped completions command |

**`[SHELL]` is OPTIONAL - both commands auto-detect from `$SHELL` env var if omitted.**

**`--shell init` is just a convenience wrapper:**
```bash
# These are equivalent:
eval "$({project_name} --shell init)"
eval "$({project_name} --shell init bash)"      # if $SHELL=/bin/bash

# init outputs the eval command, completions outputs the script:
{project_name} --shell init        # вҶ’ source <({project_name} --shell completions bash)
{project_name} --shell completions # вҶ’ (actual completion script)
```

**Supported shells:**

| Shell | Completion Support | Init Support |
|-------|-------------------|--------------|
| `bash` | Full | `source <(...)` |
| `zsh` | Full | `source <(...)` |
| `fish` | Full | `... \| source` |
| `sh` | Basic (POSIX) | `eval $(...)` |
| `dash` | Basic (POSIX) | `eval $(...)` |
| `ksh` | Basic | `eval $(...)` |
| `powershell` | Full | `Invoke-Expression` |
| `pwsh` | Full (PowerShell Core) | `Invoke-Expression` |

**Behavior:**
- `--shell completions` prints to stdout - user redirects with `> {file}`
- `--shell init` prints eval-ready command for shell rc file
- Auto-detect extracts shell name from `$SHELL` path (e.g., `/bin/bash` вҶ’ `bash`)

**Usage examples:**
```bash
# Explicit shell specification
{project_name} --shell completions bash > ~/.local/share/bash-completion/completions/{project_name}
{project_name}-cli --shell completions zsh > ~/.zsh/completions/_{project_name}-cli
{project_name}-agent --shell completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/{project_name}-agent.fish
{project_name} --shell completions powershell > ~/Documents/PowerShell/completions/{project_name}.ps1

# Auto-detect shell (omit SHELL argument)
{project_name} --shell completions > ~/completions/{project_name}
{project_name}-cli --shell init                    # auto-detect, print init
eval "$({project_name} --shell init)"              # auto-detect in eval

# Specific shell init
eval "$({project_name}-cli --shell init bash)"
eval "$({project_name}-agent --shell init zsh)"
{project_name} --shell init fish | source
```

**Add to shell rc file:**
```bash
# ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, ~/.config/fish/config.fish, etc.
eval "$({project_name} --shell init)"        # server (auto-detect)
eval "$({project_name}-cli --shell init)"    # client (auto-detect)
eval "$({project_name}-agent --shell init)"  # agent (auto-detect)
```

**Why built-in (not separate files):**

| Advantage | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Always current** | Completions match binary version exactly |
| **Rename-friendly** | Works even if user renames binary |
| **No sync issues** | Can't have outdated completion files |
| **Self-contained** | No extra files to distribute/install |

**Implementation:**
```rust
// src/cli/shell.rs
use std::path::Path;

pub fn handle_shell_command(args: &[String]) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    if args.is_empty() {
        eprintln!("Usage: --shell [completions|init] [SHELL]");
        std::process::exit(1);
    }

    let cmd = &args[0];
    let shell = if args.len() > 1 {
        args[1].clone()
    } else {
        detect_shell()
    };

    let binary_name = std::env::current_exe()
        .ok()
        .and_then(|p| p.file_name().map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().into_owned()))
        .unwrap_or_else(|| env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME").to_string());

    match cmd.as_str() {
        "completions" => print_completions(&shell, &binary_name),
        "init" => print_init(&shell, &binary_name),
        _ => {
            eprintln!("Unknown shell subcommand: {}", cmd);
            std::process::exit(1);
        }
    }
    Ok(())
}

fn detect_shell() -> String {
    std::env::var("SHELL")
        .ok()
        .and_then(|p| {
            Path::new(&p)
                .file_name()
                .map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().into_owned())
        })
        .unwrap_or_else(|| "bash".to_string())
}

fn print_completions(shell: &str, binary_name: &str) {
    match shell {
        "bash" => print!("{}", generate_bash_completions(binary_name)),
        "zsh" => print!("{}", generate_zsh_completions(binary_name)),
        "fish" => print!("{}", generate_fish_completions(binary_name)),
        "sh" | "dash" | "ksh" => print!("{}", generate_posix_completions(binary_name)),
        "powershell" | "pwsh" => print!("{}", generate_powershell_completions(binary_name)),
        other => {
            eprintln!("Unsupported shell: {}", other);
            std::process::exit(1);
        }
    }
}

fn print_init(shell: &str, binary_name: &str) {
    match shell {
        "bash" => println!("source <({} --shell completions bash)", binary_name),
        "zsh" => println!("source <({} --shell completions zsh)", binary_name),
        "fish" => println!("{} --shell completions fish | source", binary_name),
        "sh" | "dash" | "ksh" => {
            println!("eval \"$({} --shell completions {})\"", binary_name, shell)
        }
        "powershell" | "pwsh" => {
            println!("Invoke-Expression (& {} --shell completions powershell)", binary_name)
        }
        other => {
            eprintln!("Unsupported shell: {}", other);
            std::process::exit(1);
        }
    }
}
```

### --help Output

```bash
$ {project_name}-cli --help
{project_name}-cli {projectversion} - CLI for {project_name}

Usage:
  {project_name}-cli [args] [flags]
  {project_name}-cli                    # TUI mode (no args)

Options:
-h, --help                             - Show help
-v, --version                          - Show version
--shell completions [SHELL]            - Print shell completions (auto-detect if SHELL omitted)
--shell init [SHELL]                   - Print shell init command (auto-detect if SHELL omitted)
--shell help                           - Show shell integration help

--server <URL>                         - Server URL (default: from config)
--token <TOKEN>                        - API token for authentication
--token-file <FILE>                    - Read token from file
--user <NAME>                          - Target user or org (auto-detect, @user, +org)
--config <NAME>                        - Config profile name (default: cli.yml)
--debug                                - Debug output
--color <auto|yes|no>                  - Color output (default: auto)
--lang <CODE>                          - Language for output (default: auto)

Administration (requires admin token):
--admin <CMD>                          - Admin operations (--admin --help for details)
--admin server <CMD>                   - Server admin operations (--admin server --help)

  {project-specific flags listed here}

Shells: bash, zsh, fish, sh, dash, ksh, powershell, pwsh

Run without arguments for interactive TUI mode.
Run '{project_name}-cli <command> help' for detailed help on any command.
```

**If user renames binary:**
```bash
$ mypaste --help
mypaste {projectversion} - client for {project_name} API   # Shows actual binary name

Usage:
  mypaste [command] [flags]                     # Shows actual binary name
...
```

### --version Output

**MUST match server `--version` format. Shows ACTUAL binary name:**

```bash
$ {project_name}-cli --version
{project_name}-cli {projectversion} ({commit_sha}) built {build_date}

# If renamed:
$ mypaste --version
mypaste {projectversion} ({commit_sha}) built {build_date}   # Shows actual name
```

Same format as server:
```bash
$ pastebin --version
pastebin {projectversion} ({commit_sha}) built {build_date}
```

## Commands

**clients have project-specific commands only.** No standard commands required.

Example commands (project-dependent):
- `create`, `get`, `list`, `delete` (pastebin)
- `lookup`, `search`, `nearby` (airports)
- `random`, `search` (quotes)

**Notes:**
- Version and help are FLAGS (`--version`/`-v`, `--help`/`-h`), NOT commands
- No `config` command - edit `cli.yml` directly or use `--server`/`--token` flags
- No `tui` command - TUI launches automatically when no arguments provided
- Config file (`cli.yml`) is auto-created on first run with sane defaults

## Authentication

**Authentication requirements are PROJECT-DEPENDENT and ROUTE-DEPENDENT.**

| Auth Type | When Used |
|-----------|-----------|
| None | Public endpoints (e.g., `GET /api/{api_version}/pastes/{id}`) |
| API Token | Protected endpoints, user-specific data |
| Session | Admin operations (if CLI supports admin features) |

**Token Storage:**
- Stored in `cli.yml` under `server.token`
- `--token` flag saves to cli.yml only if not already set (same as `--server`)
- Environment variable: `{PROJECT_NAME}_CLI_TOKEN` (does NOT save to config)

**Priority (highest to lowest):**
1. `--token` flag (saves only if config empty/invalid)
2. `{PROJECT_NAME}_CLI_TOKEN` environment variable
3. `server.token` in cli.yml

## HTTP Client Identity

### User-Agent Rule

**The CLI binary can be renamed by users, but the User-Agent MUST always use the original project name.**

| Aspect | Uses Filename | Uses Original Name |
|--------|---------------|-------------------|
| Display in `--help` | вң“ | |
| Display in `--version` | вң“ | |
| Error messages | вң“ | |
| **Config file path** | | вң“ |
| **User-Agent header** | | вң“ |

**Why?** The server uses User-Agent to identify client types for:
- Client-specific rate limits
- Client-specific settings/features
- Analytics and usage tracking
- Version compatibility checks

### User-Agent Format

```
{project_name}-cli/{version}
```

**Examples (User-Agent uses compiled project name, not binary name):**

| Binary Name | User-Agent Header |
|-------------|-------------------|
| `{project_name}-cli` | `{project_name}-cli/1.2.3` |
| `mypaste` (renamed by user) | `{project_name}-cli/1.2.3` |
| `pb` (renamed by user) | `{project_name}-cli/1.2.3` |

### Implementation

The project name is compiled into the binary at build time:

```rust
// src/client/identity.rs
// Set at build time via build.rs env! macros or cargo-dist metadata

// Original project name (compiled in via Cargo.toml package.name or build.rs override)
pub const PROJECT_NAME: &str = env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME");
pub const VERSION: &str = env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION");

// get_user_agent returns the fixed User-Agent regardless of binary name
pub fn get_user_agent() -> String {
    format!("{}-cli/{}", PROJECT_NAME, VERSION)
}

// get_binary_name returns the actual executable name (for display)
pub fn get_binary_name() -> String {
    std::env::current_exe()
        .ok()
        .and_then(|p| p.file_name().map(|n| n.to_string_lossy().into_owned()))
        .unwrap_or_else(|| PROJECT_NAME.to_string())
}
```

**Build command (CI/CD injects version from git tag):**
```bash
# VERSION comes from git tag (see PART 26/28 for version handling)
# Cargo.toml version is set by cargo-dist / build script at release time
cargo build --release --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
```

### Server-Side Client Detection

The server can use the User-Agent to apply client-specific behavior:

```rust
// src/middleware/client_detection.rs
use axum::http::HeaderMap;

pub fn get_client_type(headers: &HeaderMap) -> &'static str {
    let ua = headers
        .get("user-agent")
        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
        .unwrap_or("");

    if ua.starts_with(&format!("{}-cli/", env!("CARGO_PKG_NAME"))) {
        return "cli";
    }
    if ua.contains("Mozilla") || ua.contains("Chrome") {
        return "browser";
    }
    "api"
}
```

## URL Encoding

**ALL user input in URLs MUST be encoded. NEVER construct URLs with raw user input.**

### URL Encoding Functions (Reusable)

```rust
// src/common/urlutil/encode.rs
use std::collections::HashMap;
use url::Url;

// build_api_url constructs a properly encoded API URL.
// ALWAYS use this function - NEVER format!() with user input in URL paths.
pub fn build_api_url(
    base_url: &str,
    path: &str,
    path_params: Option<&HashMap<&str, &str>>,
    query_params: Option<&HashMap<&str, &str>>,
) -> Result<String, url::ParseError> {
    let mut u = Url::parse(base_url)?;

    // Build path with encoded parameters
    let mut encoded_path = path.to_string();
    if let Some(params) = path_params {
        for (key, value) in params {
            let placeholder = format!("{{{}}}", key);
            encoded_path = encoded_path.replace(
                &placeholder,
                &urlencoding::encode(value),
            );
        }
    }

    let base_path = u.path().trim_end_matches('/').to_string();
    u.set_path(&format!("{}{}", base_path, encoded_path));

    // Add query parameters (properly encoded)
    if let Some(params) = query_params {
        let mut pairs = u.query_pairs_mut();
        for (key, value) in params {
            pairs.append_pair(key, value);
        }
    }

    Ok(u.to_string())
}

// encode_path_segment encodes a single path segment.
// Use for: usernames, org names, resource IDs, filenames.
pub fn encode_path_segment(segment: &str) -> String {
    urlencoding::encode(segment).into_owned()
}

// encode_query_value encodes a query parameter value.
// Use for: search terms, filter values, pagination.
pub fn encode_query_value(value: &str) -> String {
    urlencoding::encode(value).into_owned()
}

// build_query_string builds an encoded query string from a map.
pub fn build_query_string(params: &HashMap<&str, &str>) -> String {
    params
        .iter()
        .map(|(k, v)| format!("{}={}", urlencoding::encode(k), urlencoding::encode(v)))
        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
        .join("&")
}
```

### Usage Examples

```rust
// NEVER do this - injection vulnerability!
let bad_url = format!("{}/api/{api_version}/users/{}/pastes", server_url, username);

// ALWAYS do this - properly encoded
let mut path_params = HashMap::new();
path_params.insert("username", username.as_str());
let good_url = build_api_url(
    &server_url,
    "/api/{api_version}/users/{username}/pastes",
    Some(&path_params),
    None,
)?;

// With query parameters
let mut query_params = HashMap::new();
query_params.insert("q", search_term.as_str());
query_params.insert("limit", "10");
query_params.insert("lang", language.as_str());
let search_url = build_api_url(
    &server_url,
    "/api/{api_version}/search",
    None,
    Some(&query_params),
)?;

// For single segments
let path = format!(
    "/api/{{api_version}}/users/{}/pastes",
    encode_path_segment(&username)
);
```

### What MUST Be Encoded

| Input Type | Encoding Function | Example |
|------------|-------------------|---------|
| **Usernames in path** | `encode_path_segment()` | `/users/{username}` |
| **Org names in path** | `encode_path_segment()` | `/orgs/{org_name}` |
| **Resource IDs** | `encode_path_segment()` | `/pastes/{id}` |
| **Filenames in path** | `encode_path_segment()` | `/files/{filename}` |
| **Search terms** | `encode_query_value()` | `?q={term}` |
| **Filter values** | `encode_query_value()` | `?lang={lang}` |
| **Any user input** | Appropriate function | All of the above |

### Characters That MUST Be Encoded

| Character | Raw | Encoded | Risk if Not Encoded |
|-----------|-----|---------|---------------------|
| Space | ` ` | `%20` or `+` | Path confusion |
| Slash | `/` | `%2F` | Path traversal |
| Question mark | `?` | `%3F` | Query injection |
| Ampersand | `&` | `%26` | Parameter injection |
| Equals | `=` | `%3D` | Value injection |
| Hash | `#` | `%23` | Fragment injection |
| Percent | `%` | `%25` | Double encoding |
| Plus | `+` | `%2B` | Misinterpreted as space |

### API Client Implementation

```rust
// src/client/api/client.rs
use std::collections::HashMap;
use reqwest::{Client, Response};
use crate::common::urlutil::{build_api_url, encode_path_segment};

pub struct ApiClient {
    base_url: String,
    http_client: Client,
    user_agent: String,
    token: Option<String>,
}

impl ApiClient {
    pub fn new(base_url: &str, token: Option<String>) -> Self {
        let user_agent = crate::client::identity::get_user_agent();
        let http_client = Client::builder()
            .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(30))
            .user_agent(&user_agent)
            .build()
            .expect("failed to build HTTP client");

        Self {
            base_url: base_url.trim_end_matches('/').to_string(),
            http_client,
            user_agent,
            token,
        }
    }

    // get_user_resource fetches a user's resource with proper URL encoding
    pub async fn get_user_resource(
        &self,
        username: &str,
        resource_type: &str,
    ) -> Result<ApiResponse, ApiError> {
        let mut path_params = HashMap::new();
        path_params.insert("username", username);
        path_params.insert("resource", resource_type);
        let api_url = build_api_url(
            &self.base_url,
            "/api/{api_version}/users/{username}/{resource}",
            Some(&path_params),
            None,
        )?;
        self.do_api_request("GET", &api_url, None).await
    }

    // search_resources searches with proper query encoding
    pub async fn search_resources(
        &self,
        query: &str,
        limit: u32,
    ) -> Result<ApiResponse, ApiError> {
        let limit_str = limit.to_string();
        let mut query_params = HashMap::new();
        query_params.insert("q", query);
        query_params.insert("limit", limit_str.as_str());
        let api_url = build_api_url(
            &self.base_url,
            "/api/{api_version}/search",
            None,
            Some(&query_params),
        )?;
        self.do_api_request("GET", &api_url, None).await
    }

    // do_api_request performs the HTTP request with proper headers
    async fn do_api_request(
        &self,
        method: &str,
        api_url: &str,
        body: Option<serde_json::Value>,
    ) -> Result<ApiResponse, ApiError> {
        let mut req = self.http_client.request(
            method.parse()?,
            api_url,
        );

        req = req
            .header("Accept", "application/json")
            .header("User-Agent", &self.user_agent);

        if let Some(token) = &self.token {
            req = req.header("Authorization", format!("Bearer {}", token));
        }

        if let Some(json_body) = body {
            req = req
                .header("Content-Type", "application/json")
                .json(&json_body);
        }

        let resp = req.send().await.map_err(ApiError::Http)?;
        parse_api_response(resp).await
    }
}
```

## Project-Specific Commands

Each project defines its own commands based on its API.

### Smart Argument Detection

**Minimize flags. Detect intent from arguments.**

**Search/Query Services:**
```bash
# Bare args = search term (no --query flag needed)
{project_name}-cli golang tutorials        # Search for "golang tutorials"
{project_name}-cli "exact phrase"          # Quoted = exact match
{project_name}-cli --limit 5 golang        # Flags before search term OK
```

**Content/Paste Services:**
```bash
# Detection order: stdin вҶ’ file вҶ’ directory вҶ’ text
echo "hello" | {project_name}-cli          # stdin detected вҶ’ paste stdin
{project_name}-cli notes.txt               # File exists вҶ’ paste file content
{project_name}-cli /path/to/dir            # Directory вҶ’ error or list
{project_name}-cli "some text here"        # Not file вҶ’ paste as text
```

**Detection Logic:**
```rust
// src/client/detect.rs
use std::io::{self, IsTerminal, Read};
use std::fs;
use std::path::Path;

pub enum InputSource {
    Stdin(String),
    File { path: String, content: String },
    Text(String),
    Error(String),
}

pub fn detect_input(args: &[String]) -> InputSource {
    // 1. Check stdin (pipe or redirect)
    if !io::stdin().is_terminal() {
        let mut data = String::new();
        if io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut data).is_ok() {
            return InputSource::Stdin(data);
        }
    }

    // 2. If arg provided, check what it is
    if let Some(arg) = args.first() {
        let path = Path::new(arg);

        if let Ok(meta) = fs::metadata(path) {
            if meta.is_dir() {
                return InputSource::Error("error:directory".to_string());
            }
            // Is a file
            if let Ok(content) = fs::read_to_string(path) {
                return InputSource::File {
                    path: arg.clone(),
                    content,
                };
            }
        }

        // Not a file = treat as text
        return InputSource::Text(args.join(" "));
    }

    InputSource::Error("error:no-input".to_string())
}
```

**Result: Cleaner commands:**

| Instead of | Use |
|------------|-----|
| `cli create --file notes.txt` | `cli notes.txt` |
| `cli create --text "hello"` | `cli "hello"` |
| `cat file \| cli create --stdin` | `cat file \| cli` |
| `cli search --query "golang"` | `cli golang` |

**Explicit flags still work (override detection):**
```bash
{project_name}-cli --file notes.txt        # Force file mode
{project_name}-cli --text "notes.txt"      # Force text mode (not file)
```

## Build Integration

**CLI builds follow the same container-only development rules as the server. See PART 26: MAKEFILE for complete targets.**

### Local Development (Makefile + Docker)

```bash
# Quick dev build (server + CLI + agent if exist)
make dev
# Output: ${TMPDIR}/${PROJECT_ORG}/${PROJECT_NAME}-XXXXXX/{project_name}, {project_name}-cli, {project_name}-agent

# Production test build
make local
# Output: binaries/{project_name}, binaries/{project_name}-cli (with version)

# Full release (all 8 platforms)
make build
# Output: binaries/{project_name}-{os}-{arch}, binaries/{project_name}-cli-{os}-{arch}
```

### CI/CD (Direct cargo build - NOT Makefile)

```bash
# CI/CD runs inside `casjaysdev/rust:latest`, NOT actions/setup-rust or rustup
# See PART 28: CI/CD WORKFLOWS for complete examples
cargo build --release --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
```

### Directory Structure

```
{project_name}/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ src/                    # Server application
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ main.rs
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ config/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ routes/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ handlers/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ ...
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ client/             # client (if project has client)
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ main.rs         # Entry point, mode detection
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ commands/       # Command implementations
в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ root.rs     # Root command, --help, --version flags
в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ {project-specific}.rs  # e.g., create.rs, get.rs, list.rs
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ tui/            # TUI implementation (auto-launched)
в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ app.rs      # Main TUI application
в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ theme.rs    # Theme colors (dark default)
в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ views/      # View components
в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ components/ # Reusable components
в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ api/            # API client library
в”Ӯ           в””в”Җв”Җ client.rs
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Cargo.toml
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Makefile
в””в”Җв”Җ ...
```

## TUI Requirements

### Window Awareness

**CLI MUST be window-aware - detect and respond to terminal dimensions.**

```rust
// src/client/tui/app.rs
use crossterm::terminal;
use ratatui::prelude::*;

// get_terminal_size returns current terminal columns and rows
pub fn get_terminal_size() -> (u16, u16) {
    terminal::size().unwrap_or((80, 24))
}

// Handle window resize (ratatui event loop)
pub fn handle_event(app: &mut App, event: Event) {
    match event {
        Event::Resize(width, height) => {
            app.width = width;
            app.height = height;
            // Reflow content to fit new dimensions
            app.recalculate_layout();
        }
        Event::Key(key) => app.handle_key(key),
        _ => {}
    }
}
```

| Requirement | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| **Detect dimensions** | Get terminal columns/rows on startup |
| **Handle resize** | Respond to resize events, reflow content |
| **Minimum size** | Graceful degradation below 80x24 |
| **Responsive layout** | Adapt UI to available space |
| **Compact mode** | Auto-enable for small screens |

### Terminal Size Breakpoints

**Phone SSH and small terminal support is required. Many users work remotely via phone.**

**See PART 7 for `SizeMode`, `TerminalSize`, and `get_terminal_size()` definitions in `src/common/terminal/size.rs`.**

| Breakpoint | Columns | Rows | SizeMode Constant | Behavior |
|------------|---------|------|-------------------|----------|
| **Massive** | вүҘ400 | вүҘ80 | `SizeMode::Massive` | All features, full ASCII art, multiple sidebars |
| **Ultrawide** | 200-399 | 60-79 | `SizeMode::Ultrawide` | All features, full ASCII art, sidebars |
| **Wide** | 120-199 | 40-59 | `SizeMode::Wide` | All features, ASCII art, sidebar |
| **Standard** | 80-119 | 24-39 | `SizeMode::Standard` | Standard layout, simplified art |
| **Compact** | 60-79 | 16-23 | `SizeMode::Compact` | No ASCII art, abbreviated headers |
| **Minimal** | 40-59 | 10-15 | `SizeMode::Minimal` | Single column, no borders, text only |
| **Micro** | <40 | <10 | `SizeMode::Micro` | Critical info only, scrollable |

client uses `common::terminal` from PART 7:
```rust
// CLI uses common::terminal module (defined in PART 7)
use crate::common::terminal::SizeMode;

impl SizeMode {
    pub fn max_table_columns(&self) -> usize {
        match self {
            SizeMode::Micro => 2,
            SizeMode::Minimal => 3,
            SizeMode::Compact => 4,
            SizeMode::Standard => 6,
            _ => 10, // Wide, Ultrawide, Massive
        }
    }
}
```

### Responsive UI Patterns

**Tables:**
```rust
// src/client/tui/app.rs
impl App {
    fn render_table(&self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect) {
        let cols = self.size_mode.max_table_columns();
        let _ = cols;

        // Always show: ID, Name (or primary identifier)
        // Conditionally show: other columns based on space

        match self.size_mode {
            SizeMode::Micro => self.render_vertical_list(frame, area),
            SizeMode::Minimal => self.render_simple_table(frame, area, 2),
            SizeMode::Compact => self.render_compact_table(frame, area, 3),
            _ => self.render_full_table(frame, area),
        }
    }
}
```

**Help Text:**
```rust
impl App {
    fn render_help(&self) -> &'static str {
        match self.size_mode {
            SizeMode::Micro | SizeMode::Minimal => "?:help q:quit",
            SizeMode::Compact => "вҶ‘вҶ“:nav в”Ӯ enter:select в”Ӯ ?:help в”Ӯ q:quit",
            _ => "вҶ‘/вҶ“: Navigate в”Ӯ Enter: Select в”Ӯ /: Search в”Ӯ ?: Help в”Ӯ q: Quit",
        }
    }
}
```

### Minimum Features

| Feature | Required |
|---------|----------|
| Keyboard navigation | вң“ |
| Search/filter | вң“ |
| Help screen (?) | вң“ |
| Quit (q/Ctrl+C) | вң“ |
| Window resize handling | вң“ |
| Responsive layout | вң“ |
| Error display | вң“ |
| Professional appearance | вң“ |

### Required Libraries

| Library | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `ratatui` | TUI framework |
| `crossterm` | Terminal backend, events, resize |
| `tui-input` | Text input widgets |
| `crossterm` | Terminal detection/dimensions |

### Theme Support

**TUI MUST support themes per PART 16 theme rules. Uses same colors as server frontend.**

```rust
// src/client/tui/theme.rs
use ratatui::style::Color;

// TuiTheme defines ratatui colors for TUI rendering.
// Colors match ThemePalette from common::theme (see PART 16).
pub struct TuiTheme {
    pub name: &'static str,
    pub background: Color,
    pub foreground: Color,
    pub primary: Color,
    pub secondary: Color,
    pub accent: Color,
    pub error: Color,
    pub success: Color,
    pub warning: Color,
    pub muted: Color,
}

// TUI_THEME_DARK - dark theme (default) - matches server frontend ThemePaletteDark
pub const TUI_THEME_DARK: TuiTheme = TuiTheme {
    name: "dark",
    background: Color::Rgb(40, 42, 54),
    foreground: Color::Rgb(248, 248, 242),
    primary: Color::Rgb(189, 147, 249),
    secondary: Color::Rgb(98, 114, 164),
    accent: Color::Rgb(139, 233, 253),
    error: Color::Rgb(255, 85, 85),
    success: Color::Rgb(80, 250, 123),
    warning: Color::Rgb(241, 250, 140),
    muted: Color::Rgb(68, 71, 90),
};

// TUI_THEME_LIGHT - light theme (optional) - matches server frontend ThemePaletteLight
pub const TUI_THEME_LIGHT: TuiTheme = TuiTheme {
    name: "light",
    background: Color::Rgb(255, 255, 255),
    foreground: Color::Rgb(40, 42, 54),
    primary: Color::Rgb(108, 92, 231),
    secondary: Color::Rgb(99, 110, 114),
    accent: Color::Rgb(9, 132, 227),
    error: Color::Rgb(214, 48, 49),
    success: Color::Rgb(0, 184, 148),
    warning: Color::Rgb(253, 203, 110),
    muted: Color::Rgb(223, 230, 233),
};
```

**Theme is set in cli.yml:**
```yaml
tui:
  theme: dark    # dark (default) or light
```

### TUI Implementation Guidance

**Small terminal support is NOT optional. Phone SSH is a primary use case.**

**Viewport Management:**
```rust
impl App {
    pub fn calculate_layout(&mut self) {
        // Reserve space for chrome
        let header_height: u16 = 1;
        let footer_height: u16 = 1;
        let border_height: u16 = if self.size_mode >= SizeMode::Compact { 2 } else { 0 };

        self.viewport_height = self
            .height
            .saturating_sub(header_height + footer_height + border_height);
        self.viewport_width = self.width;

        if self.size_mode >= SizeMode::Wide && self.show_sidebar {
            self.viewport_width = self.width.saturating_sub(self.sidebar_width);
        }

        // Ensure minimum usable space
        if self.viewport_height < 3 {
            self.viewport_height = 3;
        }
        if self.viewport_width < 20 {
            self.viewport_width = 20;
        }
    }
}
```

**Content Truncation:**
```rust
// Truncate text to fit terminal width
pub fn truncate(s: &str, max_width: usize) -> String {
    if s.len() <= max_width {
        return s.to_string();
    }
    if max_width <= 3 {
        return s[..max_width].to_string();
    }
    format!("{}...", &s[..max_width - 3])
}

// Truncate with ellipsis in middle (for paths/URLs)
pub fn truncate_middle(s: &str, max_width: usize) -> String {
    if s.len() <= max_width {
        return s.to_string();
    }
    if max_width <= 5 {
        return s[..max_width].to_string();
    }
    let half = (max_width - 3) / 2;
